short_description: SatMAE is a self-supervised masked autoencoder framework for temporal and multi-spectral satellite imagery, leveraging temporal and spectral positional encodings and novel masking strategies to learn transferable representations for remote sensing tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) self-supervised learning
masking_strategy: Independent masking and consistent masking across temporal and spectral dimensions; typically 75% masking ratio
pretraining: Masked autoencoding with temporal and spectral positional encodings, grouping of spectral bands, and independent masking for both temporal and multi-spectral satellite imagery.
domain_knowledge: Temporal encoding using year, month, hour
Spectral grouping based on wavelength and spatial resolution
backbone_modifications: Addition of temporal and spectral positional encodings
Multiple patch embeddings for spectral groups

processing: Resize shorter side to 224, maintain aspect ratio
Resize shorter side to 224, maintain aspect ratio

pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: 90-day composites to reduce cloud coverage

short_description: TerraMind is the first any-to-any generative, large-scale multimodal foundation model for Earth observation, pretrained on dual-scale (token-level and pixel-level) representations across nine geospatial modalities. It enables generative, zero-shot, few-shot, and thinking-in-modalities applications, and achieves state-of-the-art performance on community-standard benchmarks like PANGAEA.
pretext_training_type: Masked modeling (cross-modal patch classification)
masking_strategy: Masked token reconstruction with Dirichlet sampling
pretraining: Dual-scale pretraining on pixel-level and token-level data, using masked modeling to reconstruct randomly masked tokens from random subsets of input tokens and pixel-level data.
backbone_modifications: Learnable patch-wise linear projections for pixel-level inputs

pretraining_phases.sampling: Subsampling by LULC classes and ecoregions; downsampling homogeneous regions
pretraining_phases.processing_level: Sentinel-2 L1C, L2A; Sentinel-1 GRD, RTC
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Cloud and ice annotation with SEnSeI v2
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Filtering to minimize missing data and artifacts

short_description: Clay is an open-source, multi-sensor Vision Transformer foundation model for Earth observation. It is trained using masked autoencoding and uniquely incorporates embeddings for space (Ground Sample Distance), time, and physical wavelengths, allowing it to dynamically ingest varying sensors, resolutions, and temporal gaps without structural changes to the architecture.
backbone: ViT (Vision Transformer) with dynamic spatial, temporal, and wavelength embeddings
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) self-supervised learning
masking_strategy: Random masking of patches during pretraining (default MAE strategies)
pretraining: Self-supervised masked autoencoding on a massive, diverse multi-sensor dataset spanning multiple resolutions, sensor types (optical, SAR, aerial), and global geographic regions.
domain_knowledge: Spatial resolution (Ground Sample Distance / GSD) embedding
backbone_modifications: Positional encoding combined with time and location metadata
spatial_resolution: Variable (native support for anything from 0.1m to 60m+)

pretraining_phases.dataset: Diverse multi-sensor STAC catalogs (Sentinel, Landsat, NAIP, LINZ)
pretraining_phases.token_size: patch size 8x8 (scaled internally depending on GSD)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Spatio-temporally stratified STAC sampling
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Variable
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Handled dynamically via MAE structure

short_description: SatMamba is a foundation model for remote sensing imagery that combines masked autoencoders with a State Space Model (Mamba) backbone, enabling linear computational scaling and competitive performance on high-resolution image-to-image tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) with self-supervised masked image modeling
masking_strategy: Random patch masking with 75% masking ratio; masking applied before flattening, supporting up to four scanning directions (row-major forward/backward, column-major forward/backward)
pretraining: Self-supervised masked autoencoding with Mamba backbone on high-resolution RGB satellite images (fMoW dataset), using normalized pixel values as reconstruction target and up to four scanning directions.
backbone_modifications: Multi-way Mamba block with up to four scanning directions (row-major forward/backward, column-major forward/backward)
No positional encoding or fixed positional encoding (ablation)

pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling without replacement

metrics: F1 (per class: No damage, Minor damage, Major damage, Destroyed)

short_description: SatVision-TOA is a 3 billion parameter vision transformer-based geospatial foundation model pre-trained on 100 million MODIS L1B Top-Of-Atmosphere (TOA) radiance images across 14 spectral bands. It is designed for moderate- and coarse-resolution all-sky remote sensing imagery, excelling in tasks such as 3D cloud retrieval and image reconstruction, and demonstrates strong transferability to other sensors.
pretext_training_type: Masked-Image-Modeling (MIM)
masking_strategy: Randomly mask 8x8 patches (60% masking ratio) of input image chips
pretraining: Self-supervised learning using Masked-Image-Modeling on 100 million MODIS TOA image chips (128x128x14), with SwinV2 Giant architecture, trained for 50 epochs using AdamW optimizer and bfloat16 precision.

pretraining_phases.dataset: MODIS L1B Top-Of-Atmosphere (TOA) global daily composites
regions_coverage: Global (all continents and oceans, NW, NE, SW, SE quadrants)
pretraining_phases.learning_rate: max 0.00159727 (OneCycle cosine scheduler, linear warmup, AdamW optimizer)
processing: Elliptical Weighted Averaging (EWA) for band aggregation
Selection of lowest view zenith angle
pretraining_phases.sampling: Stratified sampling across global quadrants and cover types using clustering on land- and cloud-cover structure
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: All-sky (no cloud filtering, includes cloudy and clear-sky)
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Overlapping observations: select lowest view zenith angle; only daytime images used

benchmarks.spatial_resolution: variable (ABI native, matched to MODIS 1 km)
bands_used: 14 ABI channels matched to MODIS bands

short_description: HSIGene is a foundation model for hyperspectral image (HSI) generation based on latent diffusion, supporting multi-condition control for precise and reliable HSI generation. It introduces a spatial super-resolution-based data augmentation method and a two-stage super-resolution approach to improve the perceptual quality and diversity of augmented images. HSIGene is the first and largest HSI generative model supporting multiple control conditions.
pretext_training_type: Latent diffusion with multi-condition controllable generation
pretraining: Latent diffusion model trained on real and augmented hyperspectral images with multi-condition control (sketch, segmentation, HED, MLSD, content, text). Data augmentation via spatial super-resolution using a two-stage framework (DSRNet for RGB bands, RGAN for HSI).

processing: Spectral alignment to 400-1000nm, 48 bands via linear interpolation
pretraining_phases.sampling: Cropping with stride 128

benchmarks.dataset: AID (for evaluation), Xiongan, Chikusei, DFC2013, DFC2018, Heihe (for training)

short_description: LHRS-Bot is a large multimodal language model (MLLM) tailored for remote sensing (RS) image understanding, leveraging a novel multi-level vision-language alignment strategy and curriculum learning. It is trained on large-scale RS image-text and instruction datasets constructed from global volunteered geographic information (VGI) and RS imagery.
pretext_training_type: Multi-level vision-language alignment with curriculum learning
pretraining: Three-stage curriculum learning: (1) Pre-training on LHRS-Align for vision-language alignment, (2) Multi-task pre-training on instruction datasets, (3) Supervised fine-tuning on complex instruction data.
backbone_modifications: Multi-level feature extraction from vision encoder
Vision perceiver with learnable queries and cross-attention

processing: Manual and automatic OSM key filtering
pretraining_phases.sampling: Semantic balancing and independent sampling for key-value pairs
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Images with clouds removed using a trained classification network

short_description: SpectralEarth is a hyperspectral foundation model pre-trained on a large-scale, globally distributed EnMAP dataset using self-supervised learning (SSL) algorithms (MoCo-V2, DINO, MAE) with spectral adapters integrated into ResNet and ViT backbones. It is designed for generalizable hyperspectral image analysis and evaluated on multiple downstream land cover and crop type mapping tasks.
backbone: ResNet-50, ViT-S, ViT-B, ViT-L, ViT-H, ViT-g
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning (MoCo-V2, DINO, MAE)
masking_strategy: For MAE: random masking of 90% of tokens (patches)
pretraining: Pre-trained on SpectralEarth, a large-scale EnMAP hyperspectral dataset, using SSL algorithms (MoCo-V2, DINO, MAE) with spectral adapters for spectral feature extraction.
domain_knowledge: Spectral adapter for spectral feature extraction
backbone_modifications: Replace stem layers with 1D spectral adapter (3x Conv1D+BN+ReLU)
bands: 202 EnMAP bands (after water absorption bands removed)

pretraining_phases.learning_rate: searched per experiment; StepLR for MoCo-V2, CosineAnnealingLR for DINO/MAE
pretraining_phases.sampling: Manual selection for cloud cover <10%, patchification to 128x128, non-overlapping for single timestamp, overlap for multi-temporal
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: <10% cloud coverage
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Bands with frequent missing values (water absorption) removed

short_description: GeoPix is a multi-modal large language model (MLLM) for remote sensing that extends image understanding capabilities to the pixel level, supporting image-, region-, and pixel-level tasks such as image captioning, visual question answering, visual grounding, and multi-referring segmentation. It introduces a class-wise learnable memory module and is trained with a two-stage strategy on the large-scale GeoPixInstruct dataset.
pretext_training_type: Two-stage multi-modal multi-task optimization (text generation and mask prediction)
masking_strategy: Segmentation tokens as conditioning inputs for mask prediction
pretraining: Two-stage training: Stage 1 adapts LLM to RS domain and introduces segmentation tokens; Stage 2 focuses on refining segmentation with more pixel-level data and longer training.

pretraining_phases.sampling: Stage 1: full VRSBench + GeoPixInstruct; Stage 2: 8000 VRSBench + all GeoPixInstruct subsets

benchmarks.application: Pixel-level instance segmentation in remote sensing
Pixel-level instance segmentation in remote sensing
Pixel-level instance segmentation in remote sensing

short_description: Panopticon is an any-sensor foundation model for Earth Observation, extending DINOv2 with multi-sensor view generation, spectral subsampling, and cross-attention over channels, enabling sensor-agnostic processing of multispectral, hyperspectral, and SAR data.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning with multi-view distillation (DINOv2 framework) and spectral progressive pre-training
masking_strategy: Spectral subsampling/cropping and spatial cropping; metainfo-aware masking
pretraining: Two-stage progressive pre-training: first on fMoW, then on all datasets (fMoW, SatlasPretrain, MMEarth, SpectralEarth), with spectral and spatial augmentations, cross-attention patch embedding, and spectral positional embeddings.
backbone_modifications: Cross-attention over channels in patch embedding
Wider embedding dimension in channel attention

pretraining_phases.dataset: fMoW (Functional Map of the World, including fMoW-Sentinel)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of snapshots per footprint; random channel selection

pretraining_phases.dataset: All datasets: fMoW, SatlasPretrain, MMEarth, SpectralEarth
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of snapshots per footprint; random channel selection

short_description: A self-supervised learning framework for remote sensing and geo-tagged image data that leverages spatio-temporal structure and geo-location metadata to improve representation learning, closing the gap with supervised learning on classification, detection, and segmentation tasks.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive learning (MoCo-v2) with temporal positive pairs and geo-location classification as pretext tasks
pretraining: Contrastive learning using MoCo-v2 with temporal positive pairs (spatially aligned images over time) and geo-location classification (clustering images by coordinates and predicting cluster as pretext task).
domain_knowledge: Spatio-temporal structure of remote sensing data
temporal_resolution: variable (months to years between temporal views)

short_description: SAM-Road adapts the Segment Anything Model (SAM) for extracting large-scale, vectorized road network graphs from satellite imagery, combining dense semantic segmentation with a transformer-based graph neural network for topology prediction.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised pretraining on segmentation masks (SAM pretraining)
masking_strategy: Not specified for SAM-Road; inherits from SAM pretraining
pretraining: Fine-tuned from the pre-trained Segment Anything Model (SAM) on satellite imagery for road and intersection mask prediction
domain_knowledge: Graph neural network for topology prediction

benchmarks.application: Road network graph extraction from satellite imagery
Road network graph extraction from satellite imagery

short_description: GeoRSCLIP is a domain-adapted vision-language model for remote sensing, based on CLIP and fine-tuned on the large-scale RS5M remote sensing image-text paired dataset. It leverages parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods and full fine-tuning to bridge the gap between general vision-language models and remote sensing downstream tasks.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive vision-language alignment (InfoNCE loss)
pretraining: Fine-tuning of CLIP vision-language model on RS5M, a large-scale remote sensing image-text paired dataset, using parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods (Pfeiffer, LoRA, Prefix-tuning, UniPELT) and full fine-tuning.
backbone_modifications: Adapters (Pfeiffer, LoRA, Prefix-tuning, UniPELT) for PEFT

regions_coverage: Global (with overrepresentation in North America, Europe, Asia, South America; underrepresentation in Middle/Southern Africa, Northern Canada/Russia, Southern Indonesia, Australia, Central Asia)
pretraining_phases.sampling: 5% of RS5M for validation, rest for training

short_description: UniRS is the first vision-language model unifying multi-temporal remote sensing tasks across various types of visual input, supporting single images, dual-time image pairs, and videos as input, enabling comprehensive remote sensing temporal analysis within a unified framework.
backbone: VILA-1.5 (3B) with SigLIP visual encoder and Sheared-LLaMA (3B) language decoder
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning with prompt augmentation and multi-task joint fine-tuning
pretraining: Joint instruction fine-tuning on a mixture of remote sensing datasets (GeoChat-Instruct, LEVIR-CC, ERA) with prompt augmentation and a dedicated change extraction module for dual-time image pairs.
domain_knowledge: Change Extraction module for dual-time image pairs
backbone_modifications: Addition of Change Extraction module for dual-time image pairs
Unified visual embedding representation for multi-temporal inputs

pretraining_phases.dataset: VILA-1.5 (3B) pretraining data (large-scale interleaved image-text data)

benchmarks.application: Remote sensing change detection and description

short_description: FlexiMo is a flexible remote sensing foundation model that enables adaptation to arbitrary spatial resolutions, patch sizes, and channel counts. It features a spatial resolution-aware module using parameter-free alignment embedding and a lightweight channel adaptation module leveraging prior spectral information from sensors. FlexiMo achieves strong generalization and robustness across diverse multimodal, multi-resolution, and multi-scale datasets for tasks such as scene classification, land cover classification, building segmentation, and cloud detection.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) with dynamic channel adaptation and spatial resolution-aware scaling
masking_strategy: Masked Image Modeling with spatial and channel adaptation
pretraining: Pre-trained on large-scale remote sensing datasets using a dynamic weight generator for patch embeddings and a spatial resolution-aware module for flexible adaptation.
backbone_modifications: Lightweight channel adaptation module (wavelength-guided dynamic weight generator)
bands: Variable, depending on sensor (e.g., 12-band MSI, 2-band SAR, 3-band RGB, 4-band MSI, 1-band PAN)

short_description: SwiMDiff is a self-supervised pre-training framework for remote sensing images that integrates scene-wide matching contrastive learning with a diffusion model constraint to enhance both global semantic and fine-grained feature extraction.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised contrastive learning with scene-wide matching and auxiliary diffusion constraint
masking_strategy: Pixel-level diffusion constraint (Gaussian noise addition)
pretraining: Joint training of a dual-branch contrastive learning network (MoCo-v2) with a diffusion model (DDPM) using scene-wide matching and pixel-level diffusion constraint.
domain_knowledge: Scene-wide matching to avoid false negatives from same scene
backbone_modifications: Integration of diffusion model features with contrastive encoder features at feature level

pretraining_phases.sampling: Randomly select 10000 Sentinel-2 RGB images per season (4 seasons)

benchmarks.application: Change detection in multispectral satellite imagery
Change detection in very high-resolution bitemporal imagery

short_description: Prithvi is a transformer-based geospatial foundation model pre-trained on over 1TB of multispectral satellite imagery from the Harmonized Landsat-Sentinel 2 (HLS) dataset. It is designed for generalist Earth observation tasks, including multi-temporal cloud gap imputation, flood mapping, wildfire scar segmentation, and multi-temporal crop segmentation. The model and fine-tuning workflows are open-sourced.
backbone: ViT (Vision Transformer) with 3D patch and positional embedding
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) self-supervised learning
masking_strategy: Random masking of 75% of input patches during pretraining
pretraining: Self-supervised masked autoencoding on HLS multispectral, multi-temporal satellite imagery, reconstructing masked patches using a ViT-based encoder-decoder with 3D patch and positional embeddings.
domain_knowledge: Stratified geospatial sampling based on temperature and precipitation
Cloud and missing data masking using FMask

pretraining_phases.dataset: Harmonized Landsat Sentinel-2 (HLS) L30 and S30
processing: Cloud and missing data filtering using FMask
Zarr file storage for efficient loading
Stratified sampling by geospatial statistics (temperature, precipitation)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Stratified sampling by geospatial statistics (temperature, precipitation)
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Images with <5% (cloud gap imputation) or <10% (wildfire) cloud cover; cloud and missing data filtered using FMask
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Images with missing values excluded using FMask

short_description: Cross-Scale MAE is a self-supervised masked autoencoder model for remote sensing, designed to learn robust multi-scale representations by enforcing cross-scale consistency at both structural and semantic levels using contrastive and generative losses. It leverages scale augmentation and xFormers for efficient training.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised masked autoencoding with cross-scale contrastive and generative losses
masking_strategy: Random masking of image patches after scale augmentation
pretraining: Masked autoencoding with scale augmentation, cross-scale contrastive loss (InfoNCE) in encoder, cross-scale prediction loss (MSE) and reconstruction loss in decoder

regions_coverage: Global (various regions, as fMoW is worldwide)
augmentations: Scale augmentation (random cropping and resizing to generate two scales per image)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random scale ratios selected from [0.2, 0.8]

short_description: A2-MAE is a spatial-temporal-spectral unified remote sensing pre-training method based on an anchor-aware masked autoencoder. It leverages a global-scale, multi-source dataset (STSSD) and introduces an anchor-aware masking strategy and a geographic encoding module to efficiently integrate spatial, temporal, and spectral information from diverse remote sensing imagery.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) with anchor-aware masking and geographic encoding
masking_strategy: Anchor-aware masking (AAM): dynamically adapts masking based on meta-information of a pre-selected anchor image; combines consistent, mutually-exclusive, and random masking strategies depending on source and time.
pretraining: Self-supervised pre-training on the STSSD dataset using an anchor-aware masking strategy and a geographic encoding module to reconstruct masked patches and leverage spatial, temporal, and spectral relationships.

regions_coverage: Global (over 12,000 urban centers and 10,000 nature reserves)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Clustering-based data pruning (k-means, keep hardest 10% in nature reserves)
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: ≤10% covering

short_description: FoMo-Net is a pretraining framework for remote sensing foundation models, designed to process any combination of commonly used modalities and spectral bands in remote sensing. It is trained on a diverse set of satellite and aerial datasets and evaluated on the FoMo-Bench benchmark for forest monitoring tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoding (MAE)
masking_strategy: Random spectral band selection and token masking
pretraining: Masked autoencoding with random spectral band selection, spectral and positional embeddings, and gradient accumulation, trained on a mixture of satellite and aerial datasets covering a wide range of modalities and spatial resolutions.
backbone_modifications: Single shared linear projection for all spectral bands

pretraining_phases.sampling: sampling weight 0.2
sampling weight 0.2
sampling weight 0.2
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Sentinel-2 data selected with cloud coverage below 30%

pretraining_phases.sampling: sampling weight 0.1
sampling weight 0.2
sampling weight 0.1

benchmarks.application: Land use/land cover and tree species recognition
Tree crown detection and biomass estimation

short_description: Copernicus-FM is a unified Earth observation foundation model capable of processing any spectral or non-spectral sensor modality from all major Copernicus Sentinel missions, using dynamic hypernetworks and flexible metadata encoding. It is pretrained on the Copernicus-Pretrain dataset (18.7M images) and evaluated on the Copernicus-Bench benchmark (15 hierarchical downstream tasks).
pretext_training_type: Masked image modeling (MIM) with auxiliary continual distillation
masking_strategy: Random patch masking (MAE-style), masking ratio 0.7
pretraining: Pretrained on Copernicus-Pretrain (220K grids with all modalities, 100 epochs) using masked image modeling and continual distillation from DINOv2 (RGB) and SoftCon (S1/S2). Dynamic hypernetworks generate patch embedding weights for spectral and non-spectral modalities. Metadata (location, area, time) is integrated via Fourier encoding.
domain_knowledge: Language model encoding for variable names
backbone_modifications: Dynamic patch embedding via spectral and variable hypernetworks

regions_coverage: Global land and near-land ocean, including polar regions
pretraining_phases.token_size: Varies by modality: S1/2 (16x16), S3 (8x8), S5P (4x4), DEM (64x64)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Gaussian sampling around top-10K populated cities (std=50km), uniform for polar regions, fill gaps for missing grids
pretraining_phases.processing_level: S1: GRD, S2: TOA, S3: OLCI, S5P: L2, DEM: GLO-30
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: S3: filter tiles with >20% bright pixels; S5P: filter fully NaN tiles; S1/2: not specified
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Remove corrupted/NaN-dominated images; S5P NaNs replaced with zero

short_description: SatDiFuser is a framework that adapts large-scale diffusion-based generative models for self-supervised representation learning in remote sensing, leveraging multi-stage diffusion features and novel fusion strategies to achieve state-of-the-art performance on discriminative remote sensing tasks.
backbone: DiffusionSat (latent diffusion model based on Stable Diffusion v2-1)
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning via generative diffusion (denoising diffusion process)
masking_strategy: Noise-dependent masking via diffusion process (multi-stage, multi-timestep denoising)
pretraining: Pretrained on large-scale satellite imagery using a latent diffusion model (DiffusionSat), with self-supervised denoising objective.
domain_knowledge: Handling of sensor noise and atmospheric interference
backbone_modifications: Adaptation of Stable Diffusion v2-1 to satellite imagery (DiffusionSat)
Feature fusion modules (global weighted, localized weighted, mixture-of-experts)

processing: DDIM inversion for noisy latent generation
pretraining_phases.sampling: Global-scale sampling
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Zero-filling for missing bands

benchmarks.application: Land cover and object segmentation in remote sensing

short_description: EarthMarker is the first visual prompting-based multi-modal large language model (MLLM) for remote sensing (RS), capable of interpreting RS imagery at image, region, and point levels using visual prompts (boxes and points). It employs a shared visual encoding method and a cross-domain learning strategy, and is trained on the large-scale RSVP dataset (3.65M image-point-text and image-region-text pairs).
backbone: Mixture of Visual Experts (MoV): DINOv2-ViT L/14 and CLIP-ConvNeXt
pretext_training_type: Cross-domain learning with multi-domain image-text alignment, spatial perception tuning, and RS visual prompting tuning
pretraining: Three-phase progressive learning: (1) multi-domain image-text alignment (COCO Caption, RSVP image-level), (2) spatial perception tuning (RefCOCO, RefCOCO+), (3) RS visual prompting tuning (RSVP region-text and point-text pairs) with LoRA.
backbone_modifications: Mixture of Visual Experts (MoV): combines DINOv2-ViT-L/14 and CLIP-ConvNeXt-L
Shared visual encoding for images and prompts

pretraining_phases.dataset: COCO Caption, RSVP (image-level), RefCOCO, RefCOCO+, RSVP (region/point-level)
regions_coverage: Global (various public RS datasets, Google Earth, aerial and satellite imagery)

benchmarks.application: Object classification in RS imagery with visual prompts

short_description: SpectralGPT is the first universal remote sensing foundation model purpose-built for spectral remote sensing images, using a novel 3D generative pretrained transformer (GPT) architecture. It is trained on over one million Sentinel-2 spectral images, supports progressive training on diverse datasets, and achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple downstream tasks including classification, segmentation, and change detection.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoding (MAE) with 3D generative pretraining
masking_strategy: 3D tensor-shaped spatial-spectral mask, 90% masking ratio
pretraining: Progressive pretraining on large-scale Sentinel-2 spectral image datasets (fMoW-S2 and BigEarthNet-S2), using 3D masking and multi-target reconstruction to capture spatial-spectral coupling and spectral sequentiality.

short_description: LHRS-Bot-Nova is a multimodal large language model specialized for remote sensing image understanding, featuring an enhanced vision encoder, MoE-based bridge layer, and improved instruction/caption datasets for robust vision-language alignment and spatial reasoning.
pretext_training_type: Curriculum learning: pre-training, multi-task instruction fine-tuning, supervised fine-tuning
pretraining: Three-stage curriculum: pretraining on LHRS-Align-Recap for vision-language alignment, multi-task instruction fine-tuning with LoRA, and supervised fine-tuning with spatially-focused and robust instruction datasets.
backbone_modifications: MoE-based bridge layer in vision perceiver

short_description: CRS-Diff is a controllable remote sensing image generative model based on diffusion models, supporting simultaneous text, metadata, and image condition controls for precise and high-quality RS image generation.
pretext_training_type: Diffusion-based generative pretraining with classifier-free guidance
masking_strategy: Classifier-free guidance with random omission of individual conditions (probability 0.5) and all conditions (probability 0.1)
pretraining: Two-stage: (1) Fine-tune SD 1.5 on RSICD for text-to-image; (2) Train ControlNet for multi-conditional control on fMoW and Million-AID.
domain_knowledge: Remote sensing-specific CLIP text encoder fine-tuned on RSICD
Semantic segmentation mask extraction with UNetFormer
backbone_modifications: Integration of ControlNet for multi-conditional control
Feature Fusion (FF) and Attention Feature Fusion (AFF) modules for multi-scale conditional injection
Fine-tuned CLIP ViT-L-14 text encoder on RSICD

processing: Image decoupling into HED, MLSD, Depthmap, Sketch, Roadmap, Segmentation Mask, Content, Metadata
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random combination of single or multiple conditional information

short_description: SkyEyeGPT is a unified multi-modal large language model specifically designed for remote sensing (RS) vision-language understanding, supporting open-ended RS vision-language tasks via instruction tuning with a large language model.
backbone: LLaMA2-chat (decoder-only LLM), visual encoder: EVA-CLIP ViT-G
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning (two-stage: single-task then multi-task conversation)
pretraining: Two-stage instruction tuning: Stage 1 aligns RS image-text using single-task instructions; Stage 2 fine-tunes for multi-task conversation using mixed instructions. Visual encoder is frozen; only alignment layer and LLM are fine-tuned with LoRA.
backbone_modifications: Linear alignment layer between visual encoder and LLM
spatial_resolution: variable (images standardized to 448x448 for model input)

pretraining_phases.sampling: Single-task instruction (Stage 1), then reduced single-task + multi-task conversation (Stage 2)

short_description: MATTER is a self-supervised material and texture representation learning method for remote sensing tasks. It leverages multi-temporal, spatially aligned satellite imagery over unchanged regions to learn invariance to illumination and viewing angle, enabling improved performance and faster convergence on downstream tasks such as change detection, land cover classification, and semantic segmentation.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised contrastive learning with material and texture consistency objective
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining on multi-temporal, spatially aligned Sentinel-2 imagery over unchanged regions, using patch-wise crops and contrastive learning of surface residuals.
backbone_modifications: Insertion of Texture Refinement Network (TeRN) after first layer
Surface Residual Encoder as output layer

pretraining_phases.dataset: Self-collected, orthorectified, atmospherically corrected Sentinel-2 imagery
regions_coverage: 27 regions of interest spanning 1217 km2 over three years, rural and remote regions with limited human development
pretraining_phases.sampling: Manual selection of regions, maximum 100 images per region
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Removed images with >20% cloud cover
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Removed images with <80% data coverage

short_description: RS-BYOL is a remote sensing self-supervised learning model based on the BYOL distillation architecture, designed for learning invariant representations from multispectral (MS) and SAR satellite imagery. It supports both single-channel and three-channel feature learning, leveraging multimodal data for improved downstream performance in classification and segmentation tasks.
pretext_training_type: Distillation network (BYOL-style self-supervised learning)
masking_strategy: None (uses positive pairs from MS and SAR as implicit augmentation)
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining using BYOL-style distillation on Sen12MS dataset, leveraging MS and SAR as two views for invariant feature learning. Both single-channel and three-channel models are trained.
backbone_modifications: Added MLP projection and prediction heads (2-layer, hidden size 256, batch norm, ReLU)

processing: Used both VV and VH SAR polarizations
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random selection of 50% of Sen12MS (hardware limitation)

short_description: SoftCon is a multilabel-guided soft contrastive learning framework for efficient Earth observation (EO) pretraining, leveraging land-cover-generated multilabel supervision and continual pretraining from vision foundation models. It produces multispectral and SAR foundation models that achieve SOTA results in 10 out of 11 downstream tasks.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised contrastive learning with multilabel-guided soft contrastive loss
masking_strategy: Siamese masking (randomly mask out 20% of input patches for ViT backbones during continual pretraining)
pretraining: Pretrained on SSL4EO-S12-ML, a large-scale global multilabel dataset built by matching SSL4EO-S12 Sentinel-1/2 images with Dynamic World land-cover maps. Continual pretraining from vision foundation models (DINO/DINOv2) with simple weight initialization and Siamese masking.
domain_knowledge: Cross-domain continual pretraining from vision foundation models
backbone_modifications: Separate projectors for contrastive and soft contrastive loss

pretraining_phases.learning_rate: warmed up to 1.5e-4 for 10 epochs, then cosine decay
processing: normalize 16-bit multispectral and 32-bit SAR images to 8-bit
pretraining_phases.sampling: If multiple seasons for one scene, randomly choose two for base and momentum encoder
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Images without corresponding segmentation map (due to clouds) are dropped
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Images without corresponding segmentation map are dropped

short_description: RingMoE is a 14.7-billion-parameter multi-modal remote sensing foundation model, pre-trained on 400 million images from nine satellites across four modalities (Optical, Multi-spectral, SAR-L1, SAR-L2). It uses a hierarchical Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture with modal-specialized, collaborative, and shared experts, incorporates physics-informed self-supervised learning, and supports dynamic expert pruning for efficient deployment. RingMoE achieves SOTA results on 23 out of 25 public benchmarks across six key remote sensing tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked image modeling (MIM) with physics-informed self-supervised learning
masking_strategy: Random masking of image patches; physics-informed masking for SAR-L1
pretraining: Self-supervised masked image reconstruction with modal-specific decoders and hierarchical Mixture-of-Experts encoder
backbone_modifications: Replace FFN with hierarchical Mixture-of-Experts (RMoE) layer

regions_coverage: Global (six continents, 57 countries, e.g., United States, China, Japan)
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Tiles with cloud cover automatically detected and removed
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Tiles with >30% black border discarded

short_description: LeMeViT is an efficient Vision Transformer architecture for remote sensing image interpretation, introducing learnable meta tokens and a Dual Cross-Attention (DCA) mechanism to reduce computational complexity and improve inference speed while maintaining competitive performance on classification and dense prediction tasks.
backbone: Hierarchical Vision Transformer with learnable meta tokens and Dual Cross-Attention
pretext_training_type: Supervised classification pretraining
masking_strategy: No explicit masking; uses meta tokens for sparse representation
pretraining: Pretrained on MillionAID for remote sensing scene recognition, then transferred to downstream tasks (object detection, semantic segmentation, change detection)
domain_knowledge: Spatial redundancy in remote sensing images

regions_coverage: Global (various regions, as MillionAID is a large-scale aerial dataset)
pretraining_phases.image_resolution: variable (110x110 to 31672x31672), resized to 224x224 for training
pretraining_phases.learning_rate: 5e-4 (scaled by batch size and devices, cosine decay)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random split: 1000 images per class for validation, rest for training

short_description: Segment Any Change (AnyChange) is a zero-shot change detection model for remote sensing, built on the Segment Anything Model (SAM) via a training-free adaptation method called bitemporal latent matching. It enables zero-shot prediction and generalization on unseen change types and data distributions, supporting instance-level and pixel-level change masks in fully automatic, semi-automatic, and interactive modes.
pretext_training_type: Training-free adaptation (bitemporal latent matching)
masking_strategy: Bitemporal latent matching (semantic similarity in latent space)
pretraining: Adaptation of pretrained SAM (Segment Anything Model) for change detection via bitemporal latent matching; no additional pretraining on change detection tasks.
backbone_modifications: No architecture modifications; adaptation via latent matching

benchmarks.application: Supervised object change detection (pseudo-labels from AnyChange)

augmentations: temporal-wise adjustment of brightness and contrast

short_description: CtxMIM is a context-enhanced masked image modeling (MIM) method for remote sensing image understanding. It introduces a context-enhanced generative branch to provide contextual information during reconstruction, improving representation learning for high object density remote sensing images. Pretrained on 1.28 million WorldView-3 images from Asia, CtxMIM achieves state-of-the-art results on land cover classification, semantic segmentation, object detection, and instance segmentation.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) with context-enhanced generative branch
masking_strategy: Random patch-wise masking with context-enhanced generative branch
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining on a large-scale unlabeled remote sensing dataset (1.28 million images) using masked image modeling with a context consistency constraint.

pretraining_phases.dataset: Large-scale unlabeled remote sensing dataset from WorldView-3 via Google Earth Engine
regions_coverage: Asia (cities, villages, rivers, mountains, woodlands, and other terrain)

short_description: Geographical supervision Correction (GeCo) is a method for remote sensing representation learning that adaptively corrects deviated geographical supervision using a correction matrix and regularization terms based on prior knowledge ('minor changes' and 'spatial aggregation'). It improves downstream tasks such as scene classification, semantic segmentation, object detection, and cloud/snow detection.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning with geographical supervision correction
pretraining: Network pre-training with adaptive correction of deviated geographical supervision using a correction matrix and joint optimization, guided by prior knowledge regularization.
backbone_modifications: Added a single fully connected layer as projection head

short_description: DeCUR is a multimodal self-supervised learning method that decouples common and unique representations across modalities, enhancing both intra- and inter-modal learning. It extends Barlow Twins with a loss design for modality decoupling and optionally integrates deformable attention for modality-informative feature learning. Evaluated on SAR-optical, RGB-DEM, and RGB-depth scenarios, DeCUR achieves state-of-the-art results in both multimodal and modality-missing settings.
pretext_training_type: Redundancy reduction (Barlow Twins extension), negative-free self-supervised learning
masking_strategy: Decoupling embedding dimensions into common and unique; redundancy reduction loss drives common to identity and unique to zero in cross-modal correlation
pretraining: Multimodal self-supervised learning with decoupled common and unique representations, optionally using deformable attention in ConvNet backbones
backbone_modifications: First layer modified for input channels
Deformable attention modules added to last two stages (ResNet-50)

pretraining_phases.sampling: One random season per image and modality
Cropped from 6942 scenes

benchmarks.application: SAR-optical multimodal and single-modal scene classification
RGB-DEM multimodal and single-modal semantic segmentation

short_description: OmniSat is a self-supervised multimodal fusion model for Earth Observation, capable of combining diverse EO modalities (very high resolution images, optical and SAR time series) into expressive features without labels, achieving state-of-the-art results on forestry, land cover, and crop mapping tasks.
backbone: Custom multimodal transformer with modality-specific encoders (CNN for images, LTAE for time series) and a modality-combining transformer
pretext_training_type: Cross-modal contrastive learning and multimodal masked autoencoding
masking_strategy: Random patch masking per modality
pretraining: Cross-modal contrastive learning and multimodal masked autoencoding leveraging spatial alignment of EO data
backbone_modifications: Maxpool index bypass from encoder to decoder (SegNet-style)
Lightweight Temporal Attention Encoder (LTAE) for time series

pretraining_phases.time_range: closest available year to VHR observation
pretraining_phases.sampling: All available tiles
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: No filtering; raw observations included
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Cloudy observations present; handled by attention-based date filtering

pretraining_phases.sampling: All available tiles
All available tiles

benchmarks.loss_function: Sum of cross-modal contrastive and multimodal reconstruction loss
Sum of cross-modal contrastive and multimodal reconstruction loss
Sum of cross-modal contrastive and multimodal reconstruction loss

short_description: Presto is a lightweight, pre-trained transformer model for remote sensing pixel-timeseries data, designed for computational efficiency and robustness to missing data, supporting multi-sensor and temporal inputs.
backbone: Transformer (custom, 2 layers, 8 heads, embedding size 128, MLP ratio 4)
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised masked autoencoding
masking_strategy: Structured masking: random, channel-groups, contiguous timesteps, timesteps; masking ratio 0.75
pretraining: Masked autoencoding on pixel-timeseries from multiple sensors and derived products, reconstructing masked timepoints and modalities.
backbone_modifications: Learned linear projections per channel group

pretraining_phases.dataset: Globally stratified pixel-timeseries from Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2, ERA5, NDVI, Dynamic World, SRTM DEM, Location
regions_coverage: Global (Western Hemisphere, Eastern Hemisphere split into two regions, further divided into ecoregions)
pretraining_phases.token_size: per channel group per timestep (embedding size 128)
processing: Preprocessing and export via Google Earth Engine
pretraining_phases.sampling: Stratified global sampling by ecoregion and land cover
pretraining_phases.processing_level: Sentinel-2: 10m/20m bands only (60m bands removed); SRTM DEM
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Dynamic World: only non-cloudy Sentinel-2 images; NDVI: mode of classes per month
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Robust to missing timesteps/channels via masking; static variables never masked

short_description: A multi-temporal geospatial foundation model trained on 4.2M global time series samples from NASA?s Harmonized Landsat and Sentinel-2 data archive at 30m resolution. Incorporates temporal and location embeddings for enhanced performance across diverse EO tasks. Available in 300M and 600M parameter versions.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE)
masking_strategy: Random masking of 3D spatiotemporal patches with drop mechanism for geolocation and temporal metadata
pretraining: Self-supervised masked autoencoding with 3D patch embeddings and 3D positional encodings, incorporating temporal and location metadata as weighted positional biases. Trained on global HLS time series at 30m resolution.
backbone_modifications: Addition of temporal and location metadata as weighted positional biases
Random drop of metadata during pretraining

regions_coverage: Global (excluding persistent quality issues in Greenland and Antarctica)
pretraining_phases.learning_rate: cosine scheduler, max 5e-4, linear warmup from 1e-6 for 40 epochs
pretraining_phases.sampling: Stratified by LULC and ecoregion, upsampling of urban and heterogeneous areas, temporal sequence sampling (4 timestamps, 1-6 months apart)
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Max 20% cloudy pixels per sample; sea/desert regions downsampled to 10%
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Max 1% missing pixels per band; filled by nearest interpolation

benchmarks.application: Land use/land cover, crop type, object detection
benchmarks.dataset: GEO-Bench (m-bigearthnet, m-so2sat, m-brick-kiln, m-forestnet, m-eurosat, m-pv4ger)
GEO-Bench (m-pv4ger-seg, m-chesapeake-landcover, m-cashew-plantation, m-SA-crop-type, m-nz-cattle, m-NeonTree)

benchmarks.application: Multi-temporal land cover and crop classification (EU)

benchmarks.dataset: FLUXNET, AmeriFlux, ICOS (with HLS and MERRA-2)

short_description: Seasonal Contrast (SeCo) is a self-supervised pre-training pipeline for remote sensing imagery that leverages unlabeled, uncurated Sentinel-2 data and a multi-augmentation contrastive learning method to learn representations that are both variant and invariant to seasonal changes. SeCo achieves state-of-the-art results on land-cover classification and change detection tasks.
pretext_training_type: Multi-augmentation contrastive learning (based on MoCo-v2)
masking_strategy: Multiple embedding sub-spaces for invariance/variance to seasonal and synthetic augmentations
pretraining: Self-supervised contrastive learning using both artificial and seasonal (temporal) augmentations, with multiple embedding sub-spaces for different invariances.
domain_knowledge: Sampling around populated regions for diversity
backbone_modifications: Multi-head projection heads for multiple embedding sub-spaces

regions_coverage: Global, sampled around 10k most populated cities
pretraining_phases.time_range: Images from the past year, 5 images per location separated by ~3 months
pretraining_phases.sampling: Gaussian sampling around 10k most populated cities (std=50km)
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: <10% cloud coverage
pretraining_phases.missing_data: No additional data cleaning; images with >10% cloud filtered

short_description: Text2Earth is a 1.3 billion parameter generative foundation model for global-scale, multi-resolution controllable, and unbounded remote sensing text-to-image generation. It is trained on the Git-10M dataset (10.5 million image-text pairs) and supports zero-shot text2image, image editing, unbounded scene construction, and cross-modal image generation.
backbone: Diffusion model with VAE and OpenCLIP ViT-H text encoder, U-Net with cross-attention
pretext_training_type: Diffusion-based generative pretraining with dynamic condition adaptation
masking_strategy: Dynamic condition adaptation (randomly dropping text and resolution conditions during training)
pretraining: Two-stage: initial training on full Git-10M dataset, then fine-tuning on high-quality subset (score > 4.8)
backbone_modifications: Cross-attention mechanism for text and resolution
Conditional masked image encoding for editing
supported_sensors: Public datasets: Million-AID, GeoPile, SSL4EO-S12, SkyScript, DIOR, RSICB

regions_coverage: Global (multiple continents and geographical regions, including urban areas, forests, mountains, deserts, etc.)
processing: Image enhancement (trained on private high-quality dataset)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random and manual selection for global coverage

benchmarks.loss_function: Diffusion loss (L2 loss between predicted and true noise)

short_description: GRAFT is a vision-language model for remote sensing images trained without textual annotations by aligning satellite images with CLIP's ground image encoder using large-scale ground-satellite image pairs. It enables zero-shot, open-vocabulary classification, retrieval, segmentation, and VQA for satellite imagery.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive learning with ground-satellite image pairs (unsupervised, no text)
pretraining: Satellite image encoder is trained to align with CLIP's ground image encoder using contrastive loss on large-scale ground-satellite image pairs. Pixel-level model uses patch-level alignment with ground images.
backbone_modifications: Patch embedding input dimension changed from 3 to 12 for multispectral Sentinel-2 experiments
temporal_resolution: NAIP: ~2 years revisit; Sentinel-2: ~5 days revisit
bands: Sentinel-2: B4, B3, B2 (RGB); also multispectral (12 bands)

pretraining_phases.sampling: Uniform spatial sampling, at least 112m apart, max 25 ground images per satellite image

pretraining_phases.time_range: 2017 onward (Flickr images from 2014, matched to closest Sentinel-2 image)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Uniform spatial sampling, at least 112m apart, max 25 ground images per satellite image
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Sentinel-2: <1% cloud cover

short_description: GeoText-1652 is a vision-language benchmark for natural language-guided drone geolocalization, extending University-1652 with dense image-level and region-level text annotations and bounding boxes, enabling fine-grained spatial relation matching for drone navigation and target localization.
backbone: XVLM (Swin image encoder, BERT text encoder)
pretext_training_type: Cross-modal contrastive learning with spatial relation matching
pretraining: XVLM pretrained on 16M images; fine-tuned on GeoText-1652 with spatial and grounding objectives

benchmarks.loss_function: ITC, ITM, Grounding Loss, Spatial Loss
ITC, ITM, Grounding Loss, Spatial Loss

short_description: A text-guided token pruning method for efficient vision-language understanding of large remote sensing images, introducing a Region Focus Module (RFM) and Dynamic Image Pyramid (DIP), and a new benchmark LRS-VQA with 7,333 QA pairs across 8 categories and image lengths up to 27,328 pixels.
pretext_training_type: Text-guided token pruning with attention distillation
masking_strategy: Text-guided token pruning via Region Focus Module (RFM)
pretraining: Pre-trained on 558K data from LLaVA-1.5; SFT with 484K samples including LLaVA-1.5-665K, RSVQA-HR, and template-based samples from FAIR1M-1.0, GLH-Bridge, and STAR datasets.
domain_knowledge: Dynamic image pyramid for multi-scale inference
backbone_modifications: Dynamic Image Pyramid (DIP) for multi-scale inference

pretraining_phases.dataset: 484K SFT samples (300K from LLaVA-1.5-665K, 146K from RSVQA-HR, 38K template-based from FAIR1M-1.0, GLH-Bridge, STAR)

short_description: CROMA is a remote sensing foundation model that combines contrastive and reconstruction self-supervised objectives to learn rich unimodal and multimodal representations from spatially aligned multispectral optical and SAR data. It introduces 2D-ALiBi and X-ALiBi for spatial bias in attention, enabling extrapolation to larger images and outperforming prior state-of-the-art models on multiple benchmarks.
pretext_training_type: Joint contrastive and masked autoencoding (reconstruction) self-supervised learning
masking_strategy: MAE-style random masking; independently mask 75% of radar and optical patches
pretraining: Joint pretraining of three encoders (optical, radar, and joint radar-optical) end-to-end on SSL4EO Sentinel-1 & 2 data, using contrastive and reconstruction objectives with 2D-ALiBi and X-ALiBi positional encoding.
domain_knowledge: Spatial alignment of multimodal remote sensing data
Sensor fusion (Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 multispectral optical)
Relative position encoding for spatial extrapolation
backbone_modifications: No sinusoidal position embeddings; uses 2D-ALiBi and X-ALiBi for spatial bias in attention

regions_coverage: Global, 250,000 locations sampled around the world, focused on areas surrounding human settlements
pretraining_phases.time_range: Four times per year, spread out over a year (exact years not specified)
processing: Identical augmentations applied to both modalities
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random spatial and temporal sampling; 250K locations x 4 times

benchmarks.application: Land cover and land use classification
Functional Map of the World (fMoW) classification

short_description: HyperFree is a channel-adaptive, tuning-free foundation model for hyperspectral remote sensing imagery. It uses a learnable wavelength weight dictionary to dynamically encode images with varying spectral channels and supports prompt engineering for multiple segmentation-related tasks without fine-tuning.
pretext_training_type: Prompt Engineering (P-E)
masking_strategy: Random channel selection from full spectrum during training
pretraining: Promptable training on large-scale, high-resolution hyperspectral images generated by the Hyper-Seg data engine, using random channel selection and point prompts.
domain_knowledge: Key channel selection based on expert knowledge

pretraining_phases.dataset: Hyper-Seg (generated from AVIRIS airborne sensor, plus fMoW and SpaceNet multispectral datasets)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random channel subset per iteration

short_description: USat is a unified self-supervised encoder for multi-sensor satellite imagery, based on a vision transformer with modified patch projection layers and positional encodings to handle spectral bands with varying spatial scales from multiple sensors. It is pre-trained using a masked autoencoder (MAE) approach and supports flexible input of arbitrary subsets of spectral bands from multiple sensors.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE)
masking_strategy: Inconsistent random spatial masking per band, with masking ratio 0.75 and equal ground cover masked across bands
pretraining: Self-supervised masked autoencoding on paired NAIP and Sentinel-2 imagery from the Satlas dataset, using superpositional, spectral group, and sensor encodings to model multi-sensor, multi-spectral data with varying GSDs.
backbone_modifications: Separate patch projection per spectral band
Support for arbitrary subset of bands
spatial_resolution: 1m, 10m, 20m (native GSDs for NAIP and Sentinel-2 bands)

processing: Paired NAIP and Sentinel-2 by ground area and time
pretraining_phases.sampling: Paired images by closest time, selected pairs with all required bands

benchmarks.loss_function: Mean squared error (pretraining), cross-entropy or BCE for classification

short_description: GAIR is a multimodal Geo-Foundation Model that integrates overhead remote sensing imagery, street view imagery, and geolocation data using factorized neural encoders and implicit neural representations, trained with spatially explicit contrastive learning to produce generalizable geospatial representations.
pretext_training_type: Spatially explicit contrastive learning (INCL + SECL)
masking_strategy: No masking; contrastive alignment via INR and location embedding
pretraining: Self-supervised contrastive learning aligning remote sensing, street view, and geolocation embeddings using Implicit Neural Representations and Random Fourier Features.
backbone_modifications: Added trainable linear projection layer after ViT encoders
INR module for RS encoder output

pretraining_phases.dataset: Streetscape1M (based on Global Streetscapes and Sentinel-2 imagery)
pretraining_phases.time_range: Sentinel-2 imagery from past years, one image per month
pretraining_phases.sampling: Uniform sampling of 1M street view images globally

classes: percentage of people who walk or bike

short_description: VHM is a Versatile and Honest Vision Language Model for remote sensing image analysis, trained on a large-scale image-text dataset (VersaD) with rich-content captions and an honest instruction dataset (HnstD) containing both factual and deceptive questions. It supports a wide range of remote sensing tasks and is designed to provide truthful answers.
pretext_training_type: Vision-language pretraining with image-text alignment and instruction tuning
pretraining: Two-stage: (1) Pretraining on large-scale RS image-text pairs with rich-content captions (VersaD); (2) Supervised fine-tuning on instruction datasets including factual and deceptive questions (VersaD-Instruct, VariousRS-Instruct, HnstD).
backbone_modifications: Multi-level feature integration (concatenation of 8th, 16th, 24th transformer layers)

short_description: SSL4EO-S12 is a large-scale, globally distributed, multi-temporal and multi-sensor dataset for self-supervised learning in Earth observation. It provides 251,079 locations worldwide, each with Sentinel-1 dual-pol SAR, Sentinel-2 L1C (top-of-atmosphere) and L2A (surface reflectance) multispectral images, sampled in four seasons (2020/2021), for a total of over 3 million 2640m x 2640m patches. The dataset is designed for unsupervised pre-training and benchmarking of self-supervised learning methods in remote sensing.
pretraining: Self-supervised pre-training using MoCo-v2/v3, DINO, MAE, and data2vec on Sentinel-2 L1C images (main), with multi-temporal and multimodal augmentations. Pre-training for 100 epochs on 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, batch size 256.
temporal_resolution: 4 timestamps per year (spring, summer, fall, winter)

regions_coverage: Global (sampled around top-10k populated cities, all continents except little polar coverage)
pretraining_phases.learning_rate: 0.03 (SGD for ResNet), 1.5e-4 (AdamW for ViT/DINO/MAE), 1e-3 (AdamW for data2vec)
processing: Conversion to uint8 (divide by 10000, multiply by 255)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Gaussian sampling (std=50km) around top-10k cities, 4 seasons per location
pretraining_phases.processing_level: Sentinel-1 GRD, Sentinel-2 L1C (TOA), Sentinel-2 L2A (surface reflectance)
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: <10% cloud coverage for S2
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Cloudy and overlapping patches removed before download

short_description: Scale-MAE is a scale-aware masked autoencoder for multiscale geospatial representation learning. It introduces a Ground Sample Distance (GSD)-based positional encoding and a Laplacian-pyramid decoder to learn robust representations across varying spatial scales in remote sensing imagery.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) with scale-aware positional encoding and Laplacian-pyramid decoder
masking_strategy: Random patch masking (typically 75%)
pretraining: Self-supervised masked autoencoding with GSD-based positional encoding and Laplacian-pyramid decoder to reconstruct low and high frequency components at multiple scales.
backbone_modifications: Laplacian-pyramid decoder with fewer transformer layers (3 instead of 8)

pretraining_phases.dataset: Functional Map of the World (FMoW) RGB training set
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random crop and downsampling from high-res image

short_description: RSCLIP is a vision-language foundation model for remote sensing, trained on 9.6M vision-language pairs generated without human annotation using InstructBLIP and remote sensing datasets. It uses a CLIP framework with a ViT-Base vision encoder and BERT-base text encoder, and demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on zero-shot classification, image-text retrieval, and semantic localization tasks.
backbone: ViT-Base (Vision Transformer, 12 layers, 12 heads, 16 patch size, 768 hidden size, 3072 MLP size) for vision encoder; BERT-base for text encoder
pretext_training_type: Contrastive learning (CLIP framework)
pretraining: Contrastive learning between vision and language using CLIP framework. Vision encoder and text encoder are both pretrained (vision encoder with Masked AutoEncoder on Million-AID, text encoder is BERT-base).

pretraining_phases.dataset: fMoW, Million-AID, DFC2019, DFC2021, DeepGlobe, DIOR, HRSC, Inria, RS5M

short_description: GeoKR is a geographical knowledge-driven representation learning and network pre-training method for remote sensing images. It leverages global land cover products (GlobeLand30) and geographical location as supervision for representation learning, using a mean-teacher framework to mitigate supervision noise. Pretraining is performed on the Levir-KR dataset (1,431,950 Gaofen satellite images), and the model is evaluated on scene classification, semantic segmentation, object detection, and cloud/snow detection tasks.
pretext_training_type: Knowledge-driven representation learning using global land cover products and geographical location as supervision
pretraining: Pretraining is performed by aligning image representations with knowledge representations derived from GlobeLand30 land cover proportions, using a mean-teacher framework with KL loss.

pretraining_phases.sampling: Images cut from scenes with 0.2 overlap; class balancing by duplication
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Images with cloud proportion >0.5 discarded
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Low contrast images discarded

short_description: SkySense is a large-scale multi-modal remote sensing foundation model (MM-RSFM) with 2.06 billion parameters, pre-trained on 21.5 million temporal sequences from optical and SAR data. It features a factorized multi-modal spatiotemporal encoder, multi-granularity contrastive learning, and geo-context prototype learning for universal Earth Observation imagery interpretation.
backbone: Swin Transformer (Swin-H) for HSROI, ViT-L for TMsI and TSARI
pretext_training_type: Multi-Granularity Contrastive Learning, Cross-Modal Alignment, Unsupervised Geo-Context Prototype Learning
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining with multi-granularity contrastive learning at pixel, object, and image levels, cross-modal alignment, and unsupervised geo-context prototype learning using a teacher-student framework.

pretraining_phases.dataset: Multi-modal remote sensing dataset (HSROIs from WorldView-3/4, TMsI from Sentinel-2, TSARI from Sentinel-1)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Randomly select 20 images for TMsI sequence, 10 images for TSARI sequence
pretraining_phases.processing_level: Sentinel-2 Level-2A, Sentinel-1 Level-1 IW, GRD
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Images with cloud coverage >1% filtered out (Sentinel-2)

short_description: OFA-Net is a unified foundation model for remote sensing, employing a single shared Transformer backbone and dedicated patch embedding layers for multiple data modalities with different spatial resolutions. It is pre-trained using masked image modeling on a curated multi-modal dataset and evaluated on 12 downstream tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked image modeling-based self-supervised learning
masking_strategy: Random masking of input patches for masked image modeling
pretraining: Masked image modeling on a curated multi-modal dataset with five modalities (Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2, Gaofen, NAIP, EnMAP)
backbone_modifications: Dedicated patch embedding layers for each modality

pretraining_phases.dataset: Curated multi-modal dataset (Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2, Gaofen, NAIP, EnMAP)
pretraining_phases.image_resolution: 512x512 (Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2, Gaofen, NAIP), 128x128 (EnMAP), resized to 224x224 for embedding
pretraining_phases.sampling: 10,000 samples per sub-dataset (total 50,000 for pretraining)

short_description: EarthPT is a 700 million parameter autoregressive decoding transformer foundation model for Earth Observation, trained on multispectral time series data to forecast future pixel-level surface reflectances and produce semantically meaningful embeddings for downstream tasks.
backbone: Autoregressive decoding transformer (Radford et al. 2019) with MLP input embedding
pretext_training_type: Autoregressive self-supervised forecasting
masking_strategy: None (autoregressive next-step prediction)
pretraining: Trained in an autoregressive self-supervised manner to predict the next observation in multispectral time series of surface reflectance.
domain_knowledge: Remote sensing indices (NDVI, NDWI, BSI, GCVI)
backbone_modifications: MLP input embedding for non-textual data
Additional float embeddings for date of current and next observation

pretraining_phases.dataset: ClearSky inferred Sentinel-2 equivalent imagery (UK, TL BNG tile)
pretraining_phases.time_range: January 2015 to December 2022 (train/val), January 2023 to May 2023 (test)
pretraining_phases.token_size: Each temporal observation as a token; block size 256
pretraining_phases.learning_rate: max 2E-5, decayed by factor of 10 over 1.1x total steps
pretraining_phases.sampling: Time series sampled at Sentinel-1 cadence (~5 days)
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: SAR-based, impervious to cloud (ClearSky algorithm)
pretraining_phases.missing_data: ClearSky fills gaps using SAR, so no missing data due to clouds

benchmarks.application: Pixel-level surface reflectance and remote sensing index forecasting
benchmarks.dataset: ClearSky inferred Sentinel-2 equivalent imagery (UK, TL63 BNG tile)

short_description: TEOChat is the first vision-language model (VLM) for temporal earth observation (EO) data, capable of engaging in conversations about temporal sequences of EO images. It is trained on TEOChatlas, a large instruction-following dataset with both single image and temporal EO tasks, and demonstrates strong spatial and temporal reasoning, outperforming prior VLMs and rivaling specialist models.
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning (visual instruction tuning) with next-token cross-entropy loss
masking_strategy: None (no explicit masking, uses instruction tuning)
pretraining: Instruction-tuned on TEOChatlas, which includes both single image and temporal EO tasks from multiple datasets and sensors
temporal_resolution: variable (bitemporal, pentatemporal, multitemporal up to 8 images)

pretraining_phases.dataset: TEOChatlas (GeoChat Instruct, fMoW, xBD, S2Looking, QFabric)
regions_coverage: Global (fMoW, xBD, S2Looking, QFabric, GeoChat Instruct cover worldwide locations)
augmentations: Random inclusion of image resolution and sensor name in prompt
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of up to 8 images for long sequences; random sampling of shorter sequences from QFabric

benchmarks.application: Land use/land cover classification over time
Land use/land cover classification over time
Urban development monitoring, spatial change localization
Urban development monitoring, region-based change QA

short_description: EagleVision is an object-level attribute multimodal large language model (MLLM) for remote sensing, integrating object detection and fine-grained attribute comprehension. It introduces an Attribute Disentangle module for disentangled vision tokens and is trained on the EVAttrs-95K dataset, with evaluation on the EVBench benchmark.
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning with object-level attribute understanding
pretraining: Instruction tuning on EVAttrs-95K dataset with object-level attribute annotations for ships and airplanes, using a two-stage annotation pipeline (Qwen2-VL-72B and GPT-4o) and human refinement.
backbone_modifications: Attribute Disentangle module for vision tokens
spatial_resolution: variable (FAIR1M, MAR20, ShipRSImageNet images rescaled/cropped to 1024x1024)

processing: Crop patches for airplane and ship objects
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Low-confidence or ambiguous annotations are reviewed and corrected by humans; uncertain samples are removed.

benchmarks.application: Object-level attribute understanding in remote sensing
Object-level attribute understanding in remote sensing
Object-level attribute understanding in remote sensing

short_description: RS-vHeat is a multi-modal remote sensing foundation model inspired by heat conduction, supporting optical and SAR data. It uses a heat conduction operator for efficient global feature modeling, frequency domain hierarchical masking, and multi-domain reconstruction for self-supervised pretraining. RS-vHeat achieves state-of-the-art performance and efficiency across semantic segmentation, object detection, classification, and change detection tasks on 10 datasets.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning with frequency domain hierarchical masking and multi-domain reconstruction
masking_strategy: Frequency domain hierarchical masking (20%-30% random sector mask in DCT domain) and spatial domain masking
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining on 3 million optical and SAR images using frequency domain hierarchical masking, multi-domain reconstruction, and contrastive loss in a heat-conduction-based visual encoder.
backbone_modifications: Heat Conduction Operator replaces attention/residual blocks
spatial_resolution: variable (5m for WHU-OPT-SAR, 0.5m for Potsdam, 0.3m for iSAID, 5m for Air-PolSAR-Seg)

pretraining_phases.dataset: Large-scale multi-modal dataset (450k matched optical and SAR pairs, >3 million entries)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sector masking in frequency domain

short_description: SAR-JEPA is a joint-embedding predictive architecture for self-supervised learning in SAR automatic target recognition (ATR), leveraging local masked patches and multi-scale SAR gradient features to address small targets and speckle noise in SAR imagery.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) with joint-embedding predictive architecture
masking_strategy: Local masked patches (LoMaR) with random selection of local squares and mask tokens
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining on ~100,000 SAR magnitude images from four open-source datasets, using local masked patches and multi-scale SAR gradient features as targets.

pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of local square windows for masking

short_description: XLRS-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating the perception and reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) in ultra-high-resolution remote sensing (RS) scenarios. It features the largest average image size to date, high-quality human-verified annotations, and 16 sub-tasks across three ability levels, supporting both English and Chinese.

benchmarks.task: Multimodal Perception and Reasoning (VQA, Captioning, Visual Grounding)
regions: Global (multiple cities, diverse real-world RS scenarios)

short_description: PIS is a remote sensing foundation model pretraining approach that promotes intra-instance similarity by leveraging both seasonal (temporal) and artificial augmentations to expand the positive representation space, enabling robust and generalizable feature extraction for various downstream remote sensing tasks.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning with intra-instance similarity maximization
masking_strategy: No explicit masking; expands positive representation space via seasonal and artificial augmentations
pretraining: Contrastive learning variant that expands the positive representation space by aggregating multiple intra-instance variations (seasonal and artificial) and aligning them to their centroid, with covariance regularization to prevent collapse.

processing: normalize raw 16/32-bit values by mean and std, convert to uint8
pretraining_phases.sampling: For each location, 4 seasonal timestamps; N=16 intra-instance variations (4 per timestamp)

benchmarks.application: Oriented object detection in aerial images

short_description: BAN (Bi-Temporal Adapter Network) is a universal foundation model-based adaptation framework for remote sensing change detection, leveraging frozen foundation models (e.g., CLIP, RemoteCLIP) and bridging modules to inject general knowledge into arbitrary change detection models (Bi-TAB).
pretext_training_type: Contrastive language-image pretraining (CLIP), supervised pretraining (ImageNet-21k), vision-language pretraining (RemoteCLIP)
pretraining: Foundation models are pretrained on large-scale datasets (e.g., ImageNet-21k, LAION-2B, RS5M) using contrastive or supervised objectives. BAN uses these frozen models and adapts them to CD via bridging modules and Bi-TAB.
backbone_modifications: Bridging modules for feature alignment and injection

short_description: The Original Vision (TOV) model is a self-supervised learning-based foundation model for optical remote sensing image understanding, trained in a label-free and task-independent way to generalize across scene classification, object detection, and semantic segmentation tasks.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised contrastive learning
pretraining: Two-stage human-like self-supervised contrastive learning: first on web-scale natural images for general knowledge, then on large-scale unlabeled remote sensing images for specialized knowledge, with memory retention to avoid catastrophic forgetting.

pretraining_phases.sampling: Web-crawled using 10,000 WordNet queries, max 100 images per query
Automated sampling and resampling guided by FROM-GLC10 and OSM for class balance

short_description: SenPa-MAE is a transformer-based masked autoencoder for multi-satellite self-supervised pretraining, incorporating sensor parameter encoding to enable sensor-independent Earth observation foundation models.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoding (MAE)
masking_strategy: Random masking of 66% of tokens (patch-channel tokens)
pretraining: Self-supervised masked autoencoding with sensor parameter encoding and spectral superposition augmentation across Sentinel-2, Planet SuperDove, and Landsat-8/9 imagery.
domain_knowledge: Sensor parameter encoding (spectral response function, ground sampling distance)
backbone_modifications: Sensor parameter encoding module (MLPs for spectral response and GSD)
bands: All 10m and 20m GSD bands of Sentinel-2

pretraining_phases.token_size: 9x9 patch pattern per channel, 4 channels per image
augmentations: Resolution augmentation (random cubic downsampling with Gaussian blur)
processing: Resample all images to 5m pixel-spacing grid
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of 50k patches (S2, SD), 25k patches (LS) from 29 locations; 4 random channels per image

regions: United States (9 locations, 5 for training, 4 for validation)
bands_used: 4 randomly selected channels per image from S2 or SD

regions: United States (9 locations, 5 for training, 4 for validation)

short_description: Contrastive Spatial Pre-Training (CSP) is a self-supervised dual-encoder framework for learning geospatial-visual representations from large-scale unlabeled geo-tagged images, aligning image and location embeddings for improved downstream classification tasks.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised contrastive learning between image and location embeddings
masking_strategy: Dropout-based augmentation for location encoder (SimCSE), in-batch negatives, random negative location sampling
pretraining: Dual-encoder contrastive pre-training: image encoder is frozen (pretrained on ImageNet or Geo-SSL), location encoder is trained to align with image embeddings using contrastive objectives (NCE or MC loss) with multiple negative sampling strategies.
backbone_modifications: Projection layer W() for image encoder

pretraining_phases.sampling: Stratified sampling for few-shot; random negative location sampling for contrastive
Stratified sampling for few-shot; random negative location sampling for contrastive

short_description: Generic Knowledge Boosted Remote Sensing Pre-training (GeRSP) is a pre-training framework that combines supervised pre-training on natural images and self-supervised pre-training on remote sensing images using a teacher-student architecture to learn robust and transferable representations for remote sensing downstream tasks.
pretext_training_type: Joint supervised (cross-entropy) and self-supervised (contrastive InfoNCE) pre-training
masking_strategy: Strong data augmentation (random crop, resize, color jitter, grayscale, flip, Gaussian blur)
pretraining: Teacher-student architecture with two branches: supervised pre-training on labeled natural images (ImageNet) and self-supervised contrastive learning on unlabeled remote sensing images (MAID), jointly optimized.
domain_knowledge: General visual knowledge from natural images
backbone_modifications: Shuffling batch normalization in backbone during RSCL

pretraining_phases.dataset: ImageNet (labeled natural images) + Million-AID (MAID, unlabeled remote sensing images)
regions_coverage: Global (MAID: globally distributed high spatial resolution RS images)
pretraining_phases.learning_rate: cosine annealing scheduler with restarts, lrmin=0.10, lrmax=0.01
processing: Shuffling batch normalization for contrastive branch
pretraining_phases.sampling: Equal number of natural and remote sensing images per batch

short_description: RingMo-Aerial is the first large foundation model specifically tailored for Aerial Remote Sensing (ARS) vision tasks. It introduces Frequency-Enhanced Multi-Head Self-Attention (FE-MSA), an affine transformation-based contrastive learning pre-training method, and the ARS-Adapter for efficient fine-tuning. The model achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple ARS and ApRS datasets and tasks, including semantic segmentation, object detection, scene classification, change detection, 3D reconstruction, and object tracking.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) + Contrastive Learning (CL) with affine transformations
masking_strategy: Random masking (SimMIM) for MIM; affine transformation-based positive/negative pairs for CL
pretraining: Two-stage pretraining: Stage 1 uses Masked Image Modeling (SimMIM) to capture pixel-level semantic associations; Stage 2 uses contrastive learning with affine transformations to learn ARS-specific visual features.

pretraining_phases.dataset: Large-scale composite of open-source airborne remote sensing datasets (urban, rural, wilderness, multi-season, multi-perspective)

benchmarks.application: Urban scene segmentation from UAV imagery

short_description: RS5M is a large-scale remote sensing vision-language dataset containing 5 million remote sensing images paired with English descriptions, constructed by filtering public image-text datasets and captioning label-only RS datasets with pre-trained VLMs. It is designed to enable domain-specific transfer of general vision-language models to remote sensing tasks.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive learning (InfoNCE loss), optionally with image-text matching and self-supervised loss
pretraining: RS5M is constructed by filtering 11 public image-text datasets (PUB11) using RS-related keywords and deduplication, and by generating captions for 3 large-scale RS datasets (RS3: BigEarthNet, FMoW, MillionAID) using BLIP2.
