Extensive denoising and quality assurance methods are applied, including rotation-invariant caption selection and meta-information integration.
domain_knowledge: Integration of geospatial meta-information into captions
backbone_modifications: Adapters (Pfeiffer, LoRA, Prefix-tuning, UniPELT) inserted into CLIP encoders
bands: Multispectral (up to 12 bands for Sentinel-2, 2 bands for Sentinel-1, 4/8 bands for FMoW)

regions_coverage: Global (with overrepresentation in North America, Europe, Asia, and underrepresentation in Africa, Australia, northern Canada/Russia, South America)
pretraining_phases.image_resolution: variable (average ~400x520 px, BigEarthNet 120x120 px, others up to 800x800 px)
augmentations: Image rotation (12 angles, 30 deg increments) for rotation-invariant caption selection
processing: VLM and RS image detector filtering
pretraining_phases.sampling: PUB11: top 90% VLM similarity, top 80% RS classifier; RS3: all available images from selected splits

short_description: SS-MAE is a Spatial?Spectral Masked Autoencoder for multisource remote sensing image classification, supporting joint HSI and LiDAR/SAR data. It uses spatialwise and spectralwise masking branches and a hybrid Transformer-CNN architecture for global and local feature modeling.
pretext_training_type: Masked image modeling (MIM) with spatialwise and spectralwise masking
masking_strategy: Spatialwise masking (random patches) and spectralwise masking (random spectral channels)
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining using spatial?spectral masked autoencoding on large-scale unlabeled multisource remote sensing data
backbone_modifications: Integration of lightweight CNNs (IRBs) for local feature extraction
bands: All available HSI bands per dataset

pretraining_phases.image_resolution: Varies (e.g., Berlin: 797x220, Augsburg: 332x485, Houston: not specified)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of pixels for pretraining (e.g., 200000 for Berlin, 50000 for Augsburg/Houston)

benchmarks.spatial_resolution: Matched (not specified, but both images matched)

short_description: U-BARN (Unet-BERT spAtio-temporal Representation eNcoder) is a self-supervised deep learning model for learning spatio-temporal representations from irregularly sampled multispectral Sentinel-2 satellite image time series. It combines a Unet-based spatial-spectral encoder with a transformer-based temporal encoder, and is pretrained using a BERT-inspired masked reconstruction task.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised masked reconstruction (BERT-inspired)
masking_strategy: Random permutation of spectro-spatial embedding values among selected encoded patches within a batch (temporal masking, 60% masking rate)
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining on large-scale unlabeled Sentinel-2 L2A time series from France, reconstructing masked patches from annual time series (up to 100 dates) using a shallow decoder and mean squared error loss.
backbone_modifications: Temporal attention at full spatial resolution
Removal of temporal attention from Unet bottleneck
temporal_resolution: 5-day revisit (variable, up to 100 dates per year)

pretraining_phases.dataset: Large-scale unlabeled Sentinel-2 L2A dataset (13 tiles over France, 2016-2020)
processing: Robust normalization (quantile clipping, median centering, scaling)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random selection of 10 regions of interest (1024x1024) per tile per year; temporal padding if <100 dates
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Cloudy images included; cloud mask used for loss masking only
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Invalid pixels masked in loss; temporal padding for short series

short_description: MetaEarth is a generative foundation model for global-scale remote sensing image generation. It enables the production of worldwide, multi-resolution, unbounded, and virtually limitless remote sensing images using a resolution-guided self-cascading generative framework and innovative noise sampling strategy.
pretext_training_type: Denoising diffusion probabilistic modeling
masking_strategy: Noise sampling strategy for denoising diffusion models; no explicit masking
pretraining: Trained from scratch on a large-scale, multi-resolution global remote sensing dataset collected from Google Earth, using a self-cascading conditional diffusion framework with high-order degradation for low-res simulation.
backbone_modifications: Resolution embedding via frequency encoding and MLP
Conditional concatenation of upsampled low-res features
Integration of RRDBNet encoder for conditional images

regions_coverage: Global (all continents, various land cover types)
augmentations: High-order degradation (blur, scaling, noise, JPEG compression)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of non-overlapping images at each resolution
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Images with significant cloud cover removed
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Images with noise or significant missing data removed

short_description: A generative ConvNet foundation model for remote sensing image interpretation, pretrained on the large-scale GeoSense dataset (~9 million images) using a sparse modeling and low-frequency reconstruction (SMLFR) self-supervised learning framework. Achieves state-of-the-art results on object detection, semantic segmentation, and change detection benchmarks.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised masked image modeling (MIM) with sparse modeling and low-frequency reconstruction
masking_strategy: Random masking with 0.6 masking ratio, mask patch size 32
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining on GeoSense using SMLFR: sparse modeling (masked image modeling with sparse convolution) and low-frequency reconstruction (DFT-based low-pass filtering as target)
backbone_modifications: Mask token filling for sparse maps

pretraining_phases.sampling: Random masking (mask ratio 0.6)

short_description: Pattern Integration and Enhancement Vision Transformer (PIEViT) is a self-supervised learning framework for remote sensing imagery, utilizing a teacher-student architecture with Geospatial Pattern Cohesion (GPC) and Feature Integration Projection (FIP) modules to enhance patch-level and image-level feature representation.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised masked image modeling with teacher-student architecture
masking_strategy: Random masking of input patches with mask ratio randomly chosen between 0.1 and 0.5
pretraining: Pretrained from scratch on Million-AID for 100 epochs with batch size 256, AdamW optimizer, learning rate warmed up to 1e-4, cosine decay, weight decay 0.04 to 0.4, LayerScale 1e-5, teacher momentum 0.994 to 1, data augmentations including color jittering, Gaussian blur, multi-crop, random MIM.

benchmarks.application: Object detection in optical remote sensing images

short_description: DiffusionSat is a generative foundation model for satellite imagery, based on latent diffusion models, capable of high-resolution image generation, super-resolution, temporal prediction, and inpainting, conditioned on text and numerical metadata.
pretext_training_type: Latent diffusion with metadata and text conditioning
masking_strategy: Randomly zero out metadata vector with probability 0.1 during training
pretraining: Pretrained on large, high-resolution, multi-source satellite imagery datasets with both text and numerical metadata conditioning. Encoder, decoder, and CLIP text encoder initialized from SD 2.1; only denoising UNet and metadata/timestep embeddings updated.
domain_knowledge: Metadata conditioning (latitude, longitude, GSD, cloud cover, year, month, day)
Sinusoidal projection and MLP embedding for numerical metadata
3D ControlNet for temporal and multi-spectral conditioning
backbone_modifications: 3D ControlNet extension for temporal and multi-image conditioning
Sinusoidal projection and MLP embedding for numerical metadata
bands: Sentinel-2 multispectral bands (excluding B1, B9, B10)

bands_used: Sentinel-2 multispectral (13 bands, excluding B1, B9, B10)

benchmarks.application: Predicting satellite images at arbitrary times

short_description: EarthDial is a domain-specialized vision-language model (VLM) for Earth Observation (EO) data, capable of processing multi-spectral, multi-temporal, and multi-resolution remote sensing imagery for a wide range of tasks including classification, detection, captioning, question answering, visual reasoning, and visual grounding. It is trained on the largest remote sensing instruction dataset (11.11M pairs) covering RGB, SAR, multispectral, NIR, and infrared modalities.
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning with multi-stage pretraining (conversational pretraining, RGB/temporal finetuning, multispectral/SAR finetuning)
pretraining: Three-stage pretraining: (1) conversational pretraining on large-scale remote sensing image-text pairs, (2) RGB and temporal finetuning for downstream tasks, (3) multispectral and SAR finetuning with data fusion module.
spatial_resolution: variable (supports high and low resolution, e.g., 0.5m, 10m, 30m)

pretraining_phases.dataset: EarthDial-Instruct (SkyScript, SatlasPretrain, NAIP, Sentinel-2, Landsat, Sentinel-1)
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: luminance and coverage-based filtering to remove cloudy images

short_description: GFM is a geospatial foundation model trained via a multi-objective continual pretraining paradigm, leveraging ImageNet-22k representations and a diverse geospatial dataset (GeoPile) to achieve strong performance on a variety of remote sensing tasks with minimal resource and carbon cost.
pretext_training_type: Multi-objective continual pretraining (distillation from ImageNet-22k teacher + self-supervised masked image modeling)
masking_strategy: Masked image modeling (random masking of input patches)
pretraining: Unsupervised multi-objective continual pretraining: student branch trained with masked image modeling (MIM) and distillation from a frozen ImageNet-22k teacher branch using cosine similarity of intermediate features.

short_description: OmniGeo is a multimodal large language model (MLLM) tailored for geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI), capable of processing and analyzing heterogeneous data sources including satellite imagery, geospatial metadata, and textual descriptions. It covers five core GeoAI tasks: health geography, urban geography, remote sensing, urban perception, and geospatial semantics.
pretext_training_type: Multimodal instruction fine-tuning (LoRA and full-supervision)
pretraining: Multimodal instruction fine-tuning on 12 constructed datasets covering text, RS images, street view images, POI, and geospatial vector data.

pretraining_phases.dataset: Self-constructed multimodal instruction dataset (from CDC Wonder, Gaode Maps, WorldView, Google Maps, city government websites)
pretraining_phases.time_range: 1999-2019 (for health geography); other datasets not specified
processing: Generate captions for RS and street view images
pretraining_phases.sampling: Proportional mixing of multimodal instruction data from multiple tasks

benchmarks.application: Health Geography - Dementia Death Counts Time Series Forecasting
Health Geography - Dementia Death Counts Time Series Forecasting
Urban Geography - Urban Region Function Classification

short_description: CSPT (Consecutive Pre-Training) is a self-supervised knowledge transfer learning strategy for remote sensing, leveraging vision transformers and masked image modeling to bridge domain gaps and utilize unlabeled data for downstream tasks such as scene classification, object detection, and land cover classification.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised masked image modeling (MIM)
masking_strategy: Randomly mask 75% of image patches (tokens) and reconstruct them
pretraining: Consecutive self-supervised pre-training: first on large-scale unlabeled ImageNet (IN1K), then further pre-training on task-related unlabeled remote sensing data
backbone_modifications: Encoder-decoder structure with ViT encoder and lightweight transformer decoder

pretraining_phases.dataset: Task-related unlabeled remote sensing data (e.g., AID, NR45, M-AID, DOTA, DIOR, etc.)

short_description: RSUniVLM is a unified, end-to-end remote sensing vision-language model capable of multi-level visual understanding (image-level, region-level, pixel-level) and multi-image analysis, using a novel Granularity-oriented Mixture of Experts (G-MoE) architecture. It supports tasks such as visual question answering, visual grounding, semantic segmentation, change captioning, and change detection, with about 1 billion parameters.
pretext_training_type: Instruction-following, two-stage coarse-to-fine training
masking_strategy: Semantic descriptors for segmentation masks (24x24), region bounding boxes normalized to [0,100]
pretraining: Two-stage: Stage 1 full-parameter fine-tuning on 1.2M multi-task instruction-following data (RS and general domain); Stage 2 G-MoE fine-tuning on RS-specific data split by granularity
backbone_modifications: Granularity-oriented Mixture of Experts (G-MoE) in LLM

pretraining_phases.dataset: Multi-task instruction-following dataset (RS and general domain, see Tab. 10, 11)

short_description: Aquila is a hierarchically aligned visual-language model for remote sensing, featuring a high-resolution, multi-scale vision encoder (CLIP-ConvNeXt-XXL), a Hierarchical Spatial Feature Integration (SFI) module, and a Multi-layer Deep Alignment (MDA) strategy for robust visual-language feature fusion. It achieves state-of-the-art results on remote sensing image captioning and VQA benchmarks.
pretext_training_type: Image-Text Alignment Pre-training and Instruction Fine-tuning
pretraining: Two-stage: (1) Alignment pretraining with frozen vision encoder and LLM, only SFI trainable, on 1M image-text pairs; (2) Instruction fine-tuning with LoRA on LLM, SFI trainable, vision encoder frozen, on 1.8M instruction image-text pairs.
backbone_modifications: Removed final classification layer from vision encoder
Added four projectors (2-layer MLPs) for multi-scale feature mapping

pretraining_phases.dataset: CapERA, UCM, Sydney, NWPU, RSICD, RSITMD, RSVQA-HR, RSVQA-LR, WHU_RS19 (integrated, ~1M image-text pairs)

short_description: RemoteCLIP is the first vision-language foundation model for remote sensing, trained on a large-scale, unified image-caption dataset derived from diverse remote sensing sources. It enables robust visual feature learning with rich semantics and aligned text embeddings, supporting zero-shot, few-shot, and retrieval tasks.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining (CLIP-style)
masking_strategy: None (contrastive, not masked modeling)
pretraining: Continual pretraining of CLIP on a unified, scaled-up remote sensing image-caption dataset using InfoNCE loss for vision-language alignment.
backbone_modifications: Anti-aliased rect-2 blur pooling layer added to ResNet-50
Average pooling replaced with multi-head self-attention-based pooling in ResNet-50

pretraining_phases.dataset: RET-3 (RSICD, RSITMD, UCM), DET-10 (AUAIR, CARPK, DIOR, DOTA, HRRSD, HRSC, LEVIR, RSOD, Stanford, Visdrone), SEG-4 (iSAID, LoveDA, Potsdam, Vaihingen)
regions_coverage: Global (various satellite and UAV datasets, including urban, rural, mountainous, and campus scenes)
pretraining_phases.sampling: All available images after de-duplication

benchmarks.dataset: 12 datasets (PatternNet, EuroSAT, OPTIMAL-31, RSC11, AID, MLRSNet, RSI-CB128, RSI-CB256, RESISC45, WHU-earth, WHU-RS19, RS2800)
12 datasets (PatternNet, EuroSAT, OPTIMAL-31, RSC11, AID, MLRSNet, RSI-CB128, RSI-CB256, RESISC45, WHU-earth, WHU-RS19, RS2800)
12 datasets (PatternNet, EuroSAT, OPTIMAL-31, RSC11, AID, MLRSNet, RSI-CB128, RSI-CB256, RESISC45, WHU-earth, WHU-RS19, RS2800)
12 datasets (PatternNet, EuroSAT, OPTIMAL-31, RSC11, AID, MLRSNet, RSI-CB128, RSI-CB256, RESISC45, WHU-earth, WHU-RS19, RS2800)

short_description: CMID (Contrastive Mask Image Distillation) is a unified self-supervised learning framework for remote sensing image understanding, combining contrastive learning and masked image modeling in a teacher-student self-distillation architecture. It is architecture-agnostic and effective for both CNNs and ViTs, learning representations with both global semantic separability and local spatial perceptibility.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning combining contrastive learning (CL) and masked image modeling (MIM) in a teacher-student self-distillation framework
masking_strategy: Mask patches filled with mean spectral value and add learnable mask token to patch embedding
pretraining: Teacher-student self-distillation with three branches: MIM branch (local spatial perceptibility), global branch (contrastive learning for global semantic separability), and local branch (object-level alignment via prototypes)
supported_sensors: Various sensors (MillionAID images from Google Earth, multiple sensors)

regions_coverage: Global (images from Google Earth, various locations)
pretraining_phases.sampling: All images used

short_description: DynamicVis is a dynamic visual perception foundation model for remote sensing imagery, integrating a dynamic region perception backbone based on selective state space models and a meta-embedding multi-instance learning paradigm. It is designed for efficient, scalable, and generalizable high-resolution remote sensing image understanding across diverse tasks.
backbone: Dynamic region-aware SSM backbone (Mamba-based selective state space model)
pretext_training_type: Supervised multi-instance learning with meta-embedding representations
masking_strategy: Dynamic token selection via importance scores and Gumbel noise
pretraining: Supervised pretraining on fMoW dataset using meta-embedding-based multi-instance contrastive learning, leveraging region-level annotations and categorical meta-embeddings initialized from CLIP text encoder.
domain_knowledge: Selective attention inspired by biological vision
backbone_modifications: Reduced downsampling kernel size (4 instead of 16)

pretraining_phases.dataset: fMoW-rgb (Functional Map of the World, RGB subset)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Full dataset, consolidated from train/val/test/sequence splits

short_description: RANGE is a retrieval-augmented neural field model for multi-resolution geo-embeddings, designed to improve geospatial representation learning by combining shared and unique visual features from satellite imagery and geolocation.
backbone: SatCLIP location encoder + SatMAE image encoder
pretext_training_type: Contrastive learning (CLIP objective) between geolocation and satellite images
pretraining: Contrastive alignment of geolocations and satellite images using CLIP objective, followed by retrieval-augmented fusion of high-resolution image features.
backbone_modifications: Retrieval-augmented fusion of high-resolution image features
Spatial smoothness constraint via beta parameter

pretraining_phases.sampling: Uniform sampling across globe; stratified sampling for ablation

short_description: Cross-Sensor Masked Autoencoders (CSMAEs) are adaptations of vanilla Masked Autoencoders (MAEs) for sensor-agnostic and cross-modal content-based image retrieval (CBIR) in remote sensing. CSMAEs introduce architectural and training modifications to enable learning from multi-sensor (e.g., SAR and multispectral) image pairs, supporting both uni-modal and cross-modal retrieval.
pretext_training_type: Masked image modeling (MIM) with uni-modal and cross-modal reconstruction and latent similarity preservation
masking_strategy: Random, identical, or disjoint multi-modal masking correspondence with 50% masking ratio
pretraining: Self-supervised masked image modeling on multi-sensor (SAR and multispectral) image pairs with both uni-modal and cross-modal reconstruction objectives and latent similarity preservation loss

pretraining_phases.sampling: Random selection of image pairs
Random selection of image pairs

benchmarks.loss_function: Mean squared error (uni-modal and cross-modal reconstruction), mutual information maximization loss (LMIM)
Mean squared error (uni-modal and cross-modal reconstruction), mutual information maximization loss (LMIM)

short_description: RoMA is the first self-supervised autoregressive pretraining framework for Mamba architectures in remote sensing, enabling efficient scaling to high-resolution RS imagery. It introduces adaptive rotation-aware tokenization and multi-scale token prediction to address rotational diversity, sparse targets, and extreme object scale variations in RS images. RoMA-pretrained Mamba models outperform ViT-based counterparts in accuracy and computational efficiency.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised autoregressive pretraining
masking_strategy: No masking; autoregressive next-token prediction with adaptive rotation encoding and multi-scale prediction
pretraining: Autoregressive next-token prediction with adaptive rotation encoding and multi-scale prediction strategies on large-scale unlabeled remote sensing data

short_description: TTP integrates the latent knowledge of the SAM foundation model into remote sensing image change detection, using low-rank fine-tuning, a time-traveling activation gate for bitemporal feature integration, and a lightweight multi-level decoding head. Achieves state-of-the-art results on LEVIR-CD.
pretext_training_type: Transfer learning with low-rank fine-tuning
pretraining: Leverages general segmentation knowledge from the SAM foundation model, with backbone parameters frozen and low-rank trainable parameters introduced for adaptation to remote sensing.
backbone_modifications: Low-rank trainable parameters in multi-head attention layers
Time-traveling activation gate after global attention layers

short_description: GeoCLIP is a CLIP-inspired model for worldwide image geo-localization, aligning image and GPS location features in a shared embedding space using a CLIP-based image encoder and a hierarchical location encoder with random Fourier features.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive learning (CLIP-style, SimCLR-style)
pretraining: Contrastive learning aligning image and GPS location features using a frozen CLIP ViT-L/14 backbone for images and a hierarchical MLP-based location encoder with random Fourier features and equal earth projection.
backbone_modifications: Two trainable linear layers (h1: 768, h2: 512) added after CLIP backbone
supported_sensors: RGB images (Flickr, Google Street View, etc.)

pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling from MP-16 dataset

short_description: Feature Guided Masked Autoencoder (FG-MAE) is a self-supervised learning model for remote sensing, improving MAE by reconstructing image features (HOG, NDI) instead of raw pixels, with strong performance on multispectral and SAR imagery.
pretext_training_type: Masked image modeling (MIM) with feature reconstruction
masking_strategy: Random patch masking (70%)
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining on SSL4EO-S12 using feature reconstruction (HOG+NDI for multispectral, HOG for SAR)
temporal_resolution: seasonal (4 images per location, one per season)

pretraining_phases.learning_rate: 1.5e-4 (AdamW, weight decay 0.05, warmup 10 epochs, cosine decay)
pretraining_phases.sampling: One image from a random season per location
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: No cloud filtering for SAR; not specified for MS
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Not specified

short_description: CACo (Change-Aware Contrastive) is a self-supervised learning framework for satellite imagery that leverages long-term temporal information and improved geographical sampling to learn representations sensitive to permanent changes and invariant to seasonal variations.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised contrastive learning with change-aware loss
pretraining: Contrastive learning using both short-term (seasonal) and long-term (multi-year) temporal pairs, with a conditional loss based on estimated change between long-term pairs. Improved geographical sampling focuses on informative locations near cities and avoids ocean-only samples.
domain_knowledge: Geographical sampling based on city proximity

regions_coverage: 10000 most populated cities worldwide, sampled within 10km radius, avoiding ocean
pretraining_phases.sampling: 2D Gaussian sampling around city centers, 95% within 10km

short_description: SatMAE++ is a multi-scale masked autoencoder framework for pre-training transformers on multi-spectral and optical satellite imagery, leveraging convolution-based upsampling blocks for multi-scale reconstruction. It achieves state-of-the-art results on several remote sensing benchmarks.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) with multi-scale reconstruction
masking_strategy: Random masking of 75% of patch tokens; independent masking for multi-spectral channel groups
pretraining: Multi-scale masked autoencoder pretraining on large-scale RGB and multi-spectral satellite datasets (fMoW-RGB, fMoW-Sentinel), reconstructing images at multiple scales using convolution-based upsampling blocks.
domain_knowledge: Channel grouping based on GSD for multi-spectral data
backbone_modifications: Convolution-based upsampling blocks for multi-scale reconstruction
spatial_resolution: variable (10m, 20m, 60m for Sentinel-2; high-res for fMoW-RGB)

pretraining_phases.sampling: Random crop
Random crop

short_description: DINO-MM is a self-supervised vision transformer model for joint SAR-optical representation learning in remote sensing. It extends the DINO SSL algorithm with a RandomSensorDrop augmentation to enable flexible unimodal and multimodal learning.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive self-supervised learning with knowledge distillation (DINO)
masking_strategy: RandomSensorDrop: randomly masks out SAR or optical channels of the concatenated input image
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining using DINO with vision transformer backbone and RandomSensorDrop for multimodal SAR-optical data.

pretraining_phases.learning_rate: linearly ramped up to 0.0005 over first 10 epochs, then cosine decay
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Cloud and snow artifact filtering

short_description: First billion-scale vision transformer foundation model for remote sensing, pretrained with MAE on MillionAID and evaluated on rotated object detection and semantic segmentation tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MAE)
masking_strategy: Random masking of 75% patches
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining with MAE on MillionAID dataset, using random masking and reconstruction of masked patches.

pretraining_phases.learning_rate: 0.0001 (base LR, effective LR = batch_size * base LR / 256)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Full dataset used

short_description: GeoChat is the first grounded large vision-language model (VLM) for remote sensing, capable of multitask conversational capabilities with high-resolution RS images, including image/region captioning, VQA, scene classification, visually grounded conversations, and referring detection. It is based on LLaVA-1.5 and Vicuna-v1.5, with a CLIP-ViT(L-14) visual backbone and LoRA fine-tuning.
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning with multimodal remote sensing data
pretraining: Initialized with pretrained CLIP-ViT(L-14) encoder, pretrained MLP adaptor, and Vicuna-v1.5 LLM. LoRA fine-tuning is applied to the LLM for remote sensing tasks, while the visual backbone and adaptor are frozen.
backbone_modifications: Interpolated positional encoding to support 504x504 input resolution (1296 patches)
Task-specific prompt tokens for task switching
Spatial location representation in textual format

pretraining_phases.dataset: SAMRS (DOTA, DIOR, FAIR1M), NWPU-RESISC-45, LRBEN (RSVQA), Floodnet

benchmarks.application: Grounded object detection and referring expression

short_description: HyperSIGMA is a vision transformer-based foundation model for hyperspectral image (HSI) interpretation, scalable to over 1 billion parameters. It introduces a sparse sampling attention (SSA) mechanism and a spectral enhancement module, and is pre-trained on the large-scale HyperGlobal-450K dataset (about 450,000 HSIs). HyperSIGMA provides a unified solution for both high-level and low-level HSI tasks, demonstrating superior performance, scalability, robustness, cross-modal transferability, and computational efficiency.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MAE)
masking_strategy: Random masking of spatial patches (spatial MAE) and random masking of spectral channels (spectral MAE), both with mask ratio 0.75
pretraining: Unsupervised pre-training using MAE on HyperGlobal-450K, with separate spatial and spectral subnetworks, followed by fusion via a spectral enhancement module.
backbone_modifications: SSA replaces full self-attention in most transformer blocks
Spectral enhancement module for spatial-spectral fusion
spatial_resolution: variable (depends on EO-1 and GF-5 sensors)

processing: Randomly select continuous channels for dimensionality reduction
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random channel selection, consistent number of channels per HSI
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Images selected based on cloud content

short_description: WildSAT is a remote sensing foundation model that learns satellite image representations by leveraging millions of geo-tagged wildlife observations, environmental covariates, and text descriptions of species habitats. It uses a contrastive learning framework to align satellite images, text, and location embeddings, enabling improved downstream performance and zero-shot retrieval.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive learning with multi-modal alignment (image, text, location)
pretraining: Contrastive learning aligning satellite images, text descriptions, and location/environmental covariates using wildlife observation data.

pretraining_phases.dataset: iNaturalist observations (from SINR), Wikipedia text, Sentinel-2 satellite images, WorldClim2 environmental covariates
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of text section per image-species-location match; satellite images at same location but different time for augmentation
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Significantly less cloud cover (filtered)
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Images with significant cloud cover removed; only images within 2017-2021 used

short_description: SatlasNet is a unified model for remote sensing image understanding, pre-trained on the large-scale SatlasPretrain dataset with 137 categories and 7 label types, supporting both high- and low-resolution imagery and spatio-temporal context.
pretext_training_type: Supervised multi-task pretraining
pretraining: Joint supervised pretraining on SatlasPretrain dataset with 137 categories and 7 label types, using both Sentinel-2 (low-res) and NAIP (high-res) imagery, leveraging spatial and temporal context.
backbone_modifications: Task-specific output heads for 7 label types

pretraining_phases.sampling: Maximum inverse frequency of categories per example

benchmarks.dataset: UCM, RESISC45, AID, FMoW, Mass Roads, Mass Buildings, Airbus Ships
metrics: F1 Score (FMoW, Mass Roads, Mass Buildings, Airbus Ships)

short_description: A plain Vision Transformer (ViT) model with about 100M parameters, equipped with a novel Rotated Varied-Size Attention (RVSA) mechanism, pretrained with MAE on MillionAID for remote sensing tasks including object detection, scene classification, and semantic segmentation.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoder (MAE) unsupervised pretraining
masking_strategy: Random masking of image patches with a mask ratio of 0.75
pretraining: Unsupervised MAE pretraining on MillionAID, reconstructing masked image patches
backbone_modifications: Replace MHSA with Rotated Varied-Size Attention (RVSA) in all but 3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th layers

pretraining_phases.learning_rate: not specified (AdamW optimizer, default MAE settings)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling; S1: 949848 images, S2: 51000 images (class-balanced, 1000 per class)

short_description: msGFM is a multisensor geospatial foundation model that unifies data from four key sensor modalities (RGB, Sentinel-2, SAR, DSM) using a cross-sensor masked image modeling pretraining paradigm. It is designed to handle both paired and unpaired sensor data and demonstrates strong performance across scene classification, segmentation, cloud removal, and pan-sharpening tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MIM)
masking_strategy: Random masking with 0.6 mask ratio; same mask for channels within a sensor, different mask locations across sensors
pretraining: Cross-sensor masked image modeling with separate patch embeddings per sensor, shared encoder, and separate decoders for each sensor. Incorporates Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) in encoder MLP layers. Trained from scratch on a large-scale multisensor dataset (GeoPile-2) with both paired and unpaired sensor data.
backbone_modifications: MoE replaces MLP layers in encoder
Separate patch embedding layers for each sensor

pretraining_phases.dataset: GeoPile-2 (composed of SEN12MS, MDAS, GeoPile, MillionAID)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Sequential loading of all sensor data; heterogeneous batch size proportional to data volume per sensor

short_description: SelectiveMAE is an efficient masked image modeling (MIM) method for remote sensing foundation models, pre-trained on the large-scale OpticalRS-13M dataset (13 million optical satellite images). It accelerates pre-training by dynamically encoding and reconstructing semantically rich patch tokens, reducing computational overhead while maintaining or improving downstream performance.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MIM)
masking_strategy: Progressive Semantic Token Selection (PSTS) with HOG-based semantic patch selection; 85% mask ratio, 25% reconstruction ratio
pretraining: Self-supervised masked image modeling on OpticalRS-13M, using SelectiveMAE with partial reconstruction and progressive semantic token selection
domain_knowledge: Histogram of Oriented Gradients (HOG) for semantic patch selection
Remote sensing image characteristics (sparse foreground, dense background)

regions_coverage: Global (multiple continents, diverse scenes, urban/rural, events, etc.)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling from curated public datasets, with diversity and richness criteria
pretraining_phases.processing_level: varied (depends on source, but all visible light, preprocessed to standard size)
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Not explicitly filtered; some source datasets may include cloud cover filtering
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Duplicates removed; missing/corrupt images excluded

short_description: RSBuilding is a general remote sensing foundation model for building extraction and change detection, designed to enhance cross-scene generalization and task universality. It uses a Transformer-based encoder (ViT or Swin), a multi-level feature sampler, and a cross-attention decoder with task prompts, and is trained with a federated strategy on a large-scale curated dataset.
pretext_training_type: Multi-task federated training (building extraction and change detection)
pretraining: Federated training on a curated dataset of ~245,000 images for building extraction and change detection, using both single-task and dual-task annotated data.

pretraining_phases.dataset: RSBuilding Dataset (curated from WHU, INRIA, Massachusetts, Map Challenge, Tanzania Challenge, EGY-BCD, MSBC, MSOSCD, LEVIR-CD, S2Looking, WHU-CD, BANDON, xView2)
regions_coverage: Global (including China, East Asia, US, Europe, Africa)
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Federated training strategy to handle missing task labels

benchmarks.application: Building segmentation from remote sensing imagery
Building segmentation from remote sensing imagery
Building change detection from bi-temporal remote sensing imagery
Building change detection from bi-temporal remote sensing imagery
Joint building segmentation and change detection

short_description: RingMo is a remote sensing foundation model trained with masked image modeling (MIM) on a large-scale dataset of 2 million satellite and aerial images, designed for general feature representation and state-of-the-art performance on multiple remote sensing tasks.
pretext_training_type: Generative self-supervised learning (Masked Image Modeling)
masking_strategy: Patch Incomplete Mask (PIMask)
pretraining: Self-supervised masked image modeling with PIMask strategy to preserve small and dense objects in remote sensing images.
domain_knowledge: Patch Incomplete Mask (PIMask) strategy for dense and small objects
backbone_modifications: Early convolution layers for patch embedding

pretraining_phases.dataset: RingMo RS dataset (2,096,640 images from public RS datasets and Gaofen-2 satellite)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Unsupervised collection from multiple sources

short_description: ViTAEv2-S is a vision transformer backbone pretrained from scratch on the MillionAID remote sensing dataset for aerial image understanding. It incorporates CNN inductive biases and a stage-wise design for improved locality and scale-invariance, and is evaluated on scene recognition, semantic segmentation, object detection, and change detection tasks.
pretext_training_type: Supervised classification pretraining on MillionAID
pretraining: Supervised pretraining on MillionAID (RGB aerial images, 51 classes, 949,848 training images, 51,000 validation images), using standard classification objective.
backbone_modifications: Window-based multi-head self-attention (WMHSA) in early stages
Removal of SiLU in last PCM conv layer

regions_coverage: Global (images from various countries and regions)
pretraining_phases.image_resolution: variable (110x110 to 31672x31672), resized to 224x224 for training
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling, 51,000 images (1,000 per class) for validation, rest for training

sensor: USGS National Map Urban Area Imagery Database

short_description: AIE-SEG is a large vision segmentation model for remote sensing imagery, deployed on the AI Earth cloud platform. It supports interactive annotation via text, point, or bounding box prompts, enabling batch segmentation and zero-shot extraction of objects across multiple images. The model is trained on tens of millions of remote sensing images with 1.3 billion labels, covering nearly a hundred semantic categories.
pretext_training_type: Vision-language pretraining with interactive prompts (points, boxes, text)
pretraining: Fine-tuned from SEEM using proprietary remote sensing training data (tens of millions of images, 1.3 billion labels, nearly 100 semantic categories)
backbone_modifications: Adapted SEEM for remote sensing imagery
Supports batch segmentation across multiple images

pretraining_phases.dataset: Proprietary remote sensing dataset (tens of millions of images, 1.3 billion labels)

benchmarks.application: Arbitrary target extraction, land cover classification, binary and multi-class change detection
benchmarks.dataset: Proprietary remote sensing dataset (tens of millions of images, 1.3 billion labels)

short_description: SatCLIP is a global, general-purpose geographic location encoder trained via contrastive learning to match multispectral Sentinel-2 satellite imagery with their geographic coordinates, producing 256-dimensional location embeddings for downstream geospatial tasks.
backbone: Siren (Sinusoidal Representation Network) with Spherical Harmonics positional encoding
pretext_training_type: Contrastive learning (CLIP objective) between image and location embeddings
pretraining: Contrastive pretraining aligning location encoder outputs (from coordinates) with image encoder outputs (from Sentinel-2 multispectral images) using the CLIP loss. The image encoder is frozen except for the last projection layer.
backbone_modifications: Spherical harmonics positional encoding for coordinates
Frozen image encoder except last projection layer

pretraining_phases.sampling: Uniform random sampling over global land mass
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: <20% cloud cover (ESA preprocessing)
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Reject patches with >10% nodata values

short_description: RSPrompter is a prompt learning method that augments the Segment Anything Model (SAM) for automated instance segmentation in remote sensing imagery by learning to generate category-related prompts for SAM, enabling semantically discernible segmentation results.
pretext_training_type: Prompt learning on frozen SAM backbone
masking_strategy: Prompt embedding generation (not masking in pretraining)
pretraining: Utilizes the SAM foundation model, which is pretrained on over 1 billion masks from 11 million images using a ViT-based MAE. RSPrompter itself does not pretrain from scratch but learns to prompt the frozen SAM.
backbone_modifications: Prompter module for prompt embedding generation

short_description: CrossEarth is the first vision foundation model (VFM) specifically designed for Remote Sensing Domain Generalization (RSDG) semantic segmentation. It achieves strong cross-domain generalization via an Earth-Style Injection pipeline and a Multi-Task Training pipeline, and is evaluated on a comprehensive benchmark of 28 cross-domain settings.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) and Semantic Segmentation (Multi-Task Training)
masking_strategy: Random patch masking with binary masks generated by a Mask Generator; mask ratio and patch size are hyperparameters
pretraining: Multi-task pretraining with semantic segmentation and masked image modeling using DINO-V2 backbone. Earth-Style Injection pipeline augments training data with styles from Million-AID dataset.
backbone_modifications: Integration of GSE and Injector modules

short_description: RingMo-lite is a lightweight remote sensing vision foundation model with a CNN-Transformer hybrid framework, designed for multi-task RS image interpretation. It uses a frequency-domain masked image modeling (FD-MIM) pretraining strategy to capture high- and low-frequency features, achieving state-of-the-art performance with over 60% fewer parameters than RingMo.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised masked image modeling (FD-MIM)
masking_strategy: Frequency-domain masking: 50% of patches selected, classified as high/low frequency, filtered, then random pixel masking
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining with frequency-domain masked image modeling (FD-MIM) on 150,000 visible images

pretraining_phases.dataset: 150,000 visible images (source: Google Earth, JL-1, GF-2, etc.)

short_description: SkySenseGPT is a Remote Sensing Large Multi-Modal Model (RSLMM) instruction-tuned on the large-scale FIT-RS dataset for fine-grained vision-language understanding, including complex relation reasoning and scene graph generation in remote sensing imagery.
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning
pretraining: Instruction tuning on FIT-RS (1.8M samples) and additional public datasets (scene classification, VQA, object detection, image caption, multi-turn conversation).

processing: completeness threshold (iof) 0.3 for image caption, 0.55 for other tasks
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling for train/val/test split (6:2:2)

short_description: RS-CapRet is a Vision and Language foundational model for remote sensing, capable of image captioning and text-image retrieval. It combines a frozen large language model (LLamaV2-7B) with a CLIP-based vision encoder finetuned on remote sensing image captioning datasets, using trainable linear layers to bridge modalities. The model achieves state-of-the-art or competitive results on several remote sensing benchmarks.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive language-image pre-training; cross-entropy for captioning
pretraining: CLIP vision encoder is finetuned on an aggregation of remote sensing image captioning datasets (Cap-4). The language model (LLamaV2-7B) is kept frozen. Only linear projection layers and [RET] token embedding are trained.
backbone_modifications: Trainable linear projection layers between vision encoder and language model

pretraining_phases.dataset: Cap-4 (aggregation of NWPU-Captions, RSICD, Sydney-Captions, UCM-Captions)

short_description: RS-DFM is a Remote Sensing Distributed Foundation Model enabling online collaborative perception across multiple platforms and diverse downstream tasks by mapping observations into a unified space and implementing a task-agnostic information interaction strategy. It introduces a generalized BEV generation module leveraging geometric priors and a high-low frequency decoupled collaboration module for efficient feature compression and transmission.

RingMo-Sense is a remote sensing foundation model for spatiotemporal prediction via spatiotemporal evolution disentangling. It uses a triple-branch architecture (spatial, temporal, spatiotemporal) with progressive joint training and parameter sharing, pretrained on a large-scale, diverse remote sensing spatiotemporal dataset. It achieves competitive performance on six downstream spatiotemporal tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked autoencoding with multi-branch (spatial, temporal, spatiotemporal) progressive pretraining
masking_strategy: Blockwise (spatial), Tubewise (temporal), Framewise (spatiotemporal) masking
pretraining: Progressive two-stage pretraining: first spatial and temporal branches, then add spatiotemporal branch, using self-supervised masked reconstruction on a large-scale, diverse RS spatiotemporal dataset.
spatial_resolution: variable (from high-res UAV to lower-res satellite)
temporal_resolution: variable (time-series images and videos, e.g., 15-min intervals for CloudCast)

pretraining_phases.dataset: RingMo-Sense RS Spatiotemporal Dataset (collected from multiple sources, UAV and satellite, multi-format, multi-platform, multi-scenario)
regions_coverage: Global (urban, rural, various scenes, both UAV and satellite)
processing: Format unification (mp4, avi, temporal images to avi)
Temporal frame skipping and overlap cropping (84 frames, 14 overlap)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Sliding window spatial and temporal cropping

benchmarks.application: Moving object recognition in UAV videos
Cloud type prediction from satellite time-series
Aircraft and ship detection in satellite video
Tracking aircraft and ships in satellite video
Semantic segmentation of UAV time-series images

short_description: AnySat is a multimodal Earth Observation foundation model based on a joint embedding predictive architecture (JEPA) and scale-adaptive spatial encoders, enabling training on highly heterogeneous data (resolutions, scales, modalities) in a self-supervised manner. It is pretrained on GeoPlex, a collection of 5 multimodal datasets with 11 sensors, and achieves state-of-the-art results on a wide range of downstream tasks and external datasets.
backbone: Vision Transformer (ViT) template with scale-adaptive patch encoder and modality-specific projectors
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning with Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture (JEPA) and contrastive loss
masking_strategy: Block masking (patch dropping), modality and temporal masking
pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining on GeoPlex, a curated collection of 5 multimodal EO datasets with 11 modalities, using JEPA and contrastive loss. Over 75% of parameters are shared across modalities and resolutions.
temporal_resolution: variable (from single-date to weekly time series, up to 140 time steps)

pretraining_phases.token_size: Variable, depends on patch/subpatch size and modality
processing: Random patch size selection per batch
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of dataset, patch size, and tiles per batch
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Sensor-specific scalar value for missing data; new projectors initialized for unseen sensors

short_description: Dynamic One-For-All (DOFA) is a neural plasticity-inspired multimodal foundation model for Earth Observation, capable of adaptively processing data from diverse sensors and modalities (optical, SAR, hyperspectral, etc.) within a single unified Transformer framework using a dynamic hypernetwork conditioned on spectral wavelength.
pretext_training_type: Masked image modeling with distillation loss
masking_strategy: Random masking with 75% masking ratio
pretraining: Self-supervised masked image modeling with a dynamic hypernetwork generating patch embedding weights conditioned on spectral wavelength; continual pretraining with distillation from ImageNet-pretrained teacher models.

pretraining_phases.dataset: Curated multimodal dataset (Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2, Gaofen-2, NAIP, EnMAP)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling per modality (initially 10,000 per modality, then 100,000 per modality, then full dataset)

benchmarks.application: Land cover/use, climate zone, object detection

short_description: S2MAE is a spatial-spectral masked autoencoder foundation model for spectral remote sensing data, employing a 3D transformer with a 90% masking ratio and learnable spectral-spatial embeddings. It is pretrained progressively on large-scale Sentinel-2 datasets and evaluated on classification and change detection tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MIM)
masking_strategy: 3D random masking with 90% masking ratio
pretraining: Progressive pretraining on fMoW-S2 and BigEarthNet Sentinel-2 datasets using 3D masked autoencoding with learnable spatial-spectral embeddings.

short_description: SeaMo is a season-aware multimodal foundation model for remote sensing that integrates spatial, multimodal, and multi-seasonal information using a masked image modeling framework. It employs unaligned spatial region selection, multi-source inputs, and temporal-multimodal fusion blocks to explicitly model spatial, spectral, and seasonal dimensions, enhancing generalization and robustness across geoscientific tasks.
pretext_training_type: Masked Image Modeling (MIM)
masking_strategy: Selective masking with consistent masking for simultaneous multi-sensor images and random masking for different times; partial overlap cropping across seasons
pretraining: Progressive pretraining: first on single-time multimodal data (25% of data, no TM block, 20 epochs), then on multi-seasonal multimodal data with temporal-multimodal (TM) fusion blocks (200 epochs).

pretraining_phases.time_range: 4 seasonal snapshots per location within a calendar year
pretraining_phases.sampling: Progressive: first phase uses only first season (25% of data), second phase uses all seasons
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: Images with high cloud cover excluded

short_description: RSGPT is a remote sensing vision-language model based on finetuning InstructBLIP on a high-quality, human-annotated remote sensing image captioning dataset (RSICap). It aligns visual features of remote sensing images with large language models for tasks such as image captioning and visual question answering, outperforming previous state-of-the-art methods.
backbone: InstructBLIP (frozen image encoder and LLM, Q-Former and linear layer finetuned)
pretext_training_type: Instruction-tuned vision-language alignment (finetuning Q-Former and linear layer with instruction prompts)
pretraining: Finetuning InstructBLIP on RSICap (2,585 human-annotated image-caption pairs) for remote sensing domain adaptation. Only Q-Former and linear layer are trained; image encoder and LLM are frozen.
backbone_modifications: Finetune only Q-Former and linear layer; freeze image encoder and LLM

pretraining_phases.sampling: Random selection of 2585 patches from DOTA-v1.5

short_description: DINO-MC is a self-supervised learning model for remote sensing imagery that extends DINO by using multi-sized local crops and color transformations to improve representation learning. It achieves state-of-the-art results on several remote sensing benchmarks using only 10% of the pretraining data.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive self-supervised learning with knowledge distillation and multi-sized cropping
masking_strategy: Multi-sized local crops instead of fixed-size local crops
pretraining: Self-supervised pre-training on SeCo-100K using DINO-MC with multi-sized crops and color augmentations; temporal views not used as positives in DINO-MC
backbone_modifications: Different color augmentations for global and local crops

short_description: RingMoGPT is a unified multimodal remote sensing foundation model integrating vision, language, and localization tasks. It features a location- and instruction-aware Q-Former and a change detection module, trained on large-scale high-quality image-text and instruction-tuning datasets, supporting tasks such as scene classification, object detection, VQA, image captioning, grounded captioning, and change captioning.

pretext_training_type: Adapter-based domain adaptation with frozen visual encoder and LLM, training only adapter parameters
masking_strategy: Masked image modeling and instruction-based masking
pretraining: Pretrained on 522,769 high-quality RS image-text pairs with rule-based and VLM-generated captions, followed by instruction-tuning on 1.6M QA pairs across six tasks.

Graph Attention Networks in cross-attention module

pretraining_phases.sampling: Random and category-balanced sampling from public RS datasets

short_description: Falcon is a holistic vision-language foundation model for remote sensing, supporting 14 tasks at image, region, and pixel levels. It uses a unified prompt-based architecture and is trained on the large-scale Falcon SFT dataset.
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning (multi-task, multi-instruction)
masking_strategy: None (sequence-to-sequence with prompt variation)
pretraining: Unified multi-task instruction tuning on Falcon SFT dataset with dynamic prompt strategy and cross-entropy loss.

pretraining_phases.sampling: Manual screening and integration of 67 datasets

Visual question answering for remote sensing
Object detection with horizontal bounding boxes
Object detection with oriented bounding boxes

short_description: EarthGPT is a pioneering multi-modal large language model (MLLM) designed for universal remote sensing (RS) image comprehension. It unifies multi-sensor and diverse RS interpretation tasks, including scene classification, image captioning, region-level captioning, visual question answering (VQA), visual grounding, and object detection, across optical, SAR, and infrared modalities. It introduces a visual-enhanced perception mechanism, cross-modal mutual comprehension, and unified instruction tuning, and is trained on the large-scale MMRS-1M dataset (over 1M image-text pairs from 34 RS datasets).
backbone: LLaMA-2 (LLM), DINOv2 ViT-L/14 (ViT encoder), CLIP ConvNeXt-L (CNN encoder)
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning, cross-modal mutual comprehension, visual-language alignment
pretraining: Stage 1: Visual-enhanced perception (ViT and CNN frozen, extract multi-layer and multi-scale features); Stage 2: Cross-modal mutual comprehension (unfrozen self-attention and RMSNorm in LLaMA-2, trained on LAION-400M and COCO Caption for vision-language alignment); Stage 3: Unified instruction tuning for RS (bias tuning of linear layers in LLaMA-2, trained on MMRS-1M for multi-task RS instruction following)
backbone_modifications: Visual-enhanced perception mechanism (mixing ViT and CNN backbones)
Unfreezing self-attention and RMSNorm layers for cross-modal alignment
Bias and shift tuning in LLaMA-2 linear layers

short_description: RS-LLaVA is a large vision-language model for joint captioning and question answering in remote sensing imagery. It adapts the LLaVA architecture for remote sensing via LoRA fine-tuning and is trained on a multi-task RS-instructions dataset.
pretext_training_type: Instruction tuning with LoRA
pretraining: Two-step: pre-train projection network between frozen image encoder and LLM on general image-language data, then LoRA-based fine-tuning of LLM on RS-instructions dataset.
backbone_modifications: Added projection network between image encoder and LLM

pretraining_phases.dataset: General image-language dataset for text?image pairs (not RS-specific)

short_description: REO-VLM is a unified vision-language model for Earth Observation that integrates regression and generative capabilities, enabling both scientific regression (e.g., above-ground biomass estimation) and image content interpretation from multimodal EO data. It is trained and evaluated on the REO-Instruct benchmark, which contains 1.6 million EO-language pairs with RGB, multispectral, and SAR imagery.
pretext_training_type: Two-stage: first stage for generation (cross-entropy loss), second stage for regression (MSE loss)
pretraining: Two-stage training: (1) fine-tune LLM and generation head with cross-entropy loss; (2) fine-tune regression head and reverse projection module with MSE loss. Visual encoder and multimodal projector are frozen.
backbone_modifications: Spectral recombination and pseudo-RGB strategies for MS and SAR alignment

regions_coverage: Global (various land cover types, geographic distributions)
processing: Spectral recombination to 5 pseudo-RGB images per MS image
pretraining_phases.sampling: Weighted selection and resampling for land cover balance

benchmarks.application: Land cover mapping from EO imagery

short_description: OReole is a family of billion-parameter Vision Transformer (ViT) foundation models for high-resolution satellite imagery, pretrained using masked autoencoding on diverse, large-scale remote sensing datasets. It includes OReole-MR (medium-resolution, RGB) and OReole-HR (high-resolution, BGR+NIR) variants, with public release of models, code, and datasets planned.
pretext_training_type: Masked Autoencoding (MAE)
masking_strategy: Random patch masking
pretraining: MAE pretraining on large, diverse remote sensing datasets (MillionAID for OReole-MR, TIU and ORB for OReole-HR), using FSDP-enabled distributed training on HPC resources.
domain_knowledge: Population density, land cover, climate zone, and biome-informed sampling
Transfer learning from RGB to BGR+NIR

pretraining_phases.sampling: Diverse sampling from Google Earth multisensor data
Random sampling
Diversity sampling by geography, temporality, and sensor

benchmarks.application: Object detection in remote sensing imagery
Oriented object detection in remote sensing imagery

short_description: MP-MAE is a Multi-Pretext Masked Autoencoder for geospatial representation learning, pretrained on the global, multi-modal MMEarth dataset (1.2M locations, 12 modalities). It extends ConvNeXt V2 MAE with multi-modal pretext tasks for learning general-purpose representations for Sentinel-2 optical images.
pretext_training_type: Multi-modal masked autoencoding with multi-pretext tasks (pixel-level and image-level modalities)
masking_strategy: Random masking of input patches (16x16 or 8x8) with learnable mask token; same mask applied to all pixel-level modalities
pretraining: Masked autoencoding with multi-modal reconstruction tasks (pixel-level and image-level) using MMEarth dataset. Task-uncertainty weighted multi-task loss.
domain_knowledge: Cyclic encoding of geolocation and date
backbone_modifications: Reduced patch size (from 32 to 16 or 8) for medium-resolution satellite images
Modified first layer to avoid early downsampling (kernel size 3, stride 1)
Initial convolutional layer at input resolution followed by depth-wise downsampling

processing: Standardization (zero mean, unit variance) per channel
Missing input pixels replaced by zero
Local patch normalization for optical bands
pretraining_phases.sampling: Biome-balanced, stratified by ecoregion, uniform across 14 biomes and 4 years
pretraining_phases.cloud_cover: CLOUD_PIXEL_PERCENTAGE < 10% at tile level
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Missing input pixels replaced by zero; Dynamic World NO_DATA handled as class 0; no sample-level cloud filtering

short_description: MM-VSF is a knowledge-guided multimodal spatiotemporal foundation model for remote sensing, using both satellite spectral imagery and weather data with a variable step forecasting pretraining objective. It produces temporally flexible, robust embeddings for downstream tasks such as crop mapping.
backbone: Vision Transformer (ViT) encoder, BERT-style temporal transformer, BiLSTM for weather
pretext_training_type: Variable Step Forecasting (VSF)
masking_strategy: Spatiotemporally uniform masking (equal number of masked patches per timestamp and per spatial patch location)
pretraining: Multimodal forecasting: predict a future spectral image using a series of past spectral images and weather data up to the forecast date. Masking is applied to spectral imagery.
domain_knowledge: Knowledge-guided: leverages physical drivers (weather) and their impact on environmental systems
backbone_modifications: Shared ViT encoder for all timestamps
temporal_resolution: variable (Sentinel-2: up to 70/year in US, 40/year in India; ERA5 Land: daily)

processing: Collect all Sentinel-2 images for each region in the year
pretraining_phases.sampling: Random sampling of 10000 locations globally
pretraining_phases.missing_data: Regions with missing data or improper coverage have fewer samples

benchmarks.dataset: Sentinel-2 + ERA5 Land (T11SKA tile, California Central Valley), Cropland Data Layer (CDL) labels
regions: T11SKA tile, California Central Valley, USA

short_description: GeoGround is a unified large vision-language model for remote sensing visual grounding, supporting horizontal bounding box (HBB), oriented bounding box (OBB), and segmentation mask outputs via a text-based approach. It introduces the Text-Mask paradigm and hybrid supervision, and is trained on the large-scale refGeo dataset.
pretext_training_type: Text regression loss with hybrid supervision (Text-HBB, Text-OBB, Text-Mask)
masking_strategy: Textualization of mask via downsampling and R-RLE compression
pretraining: Unified training pipeline using textualized signals (Text-HBB, Text-OBB, Text-Mask) and hybrid supervision (prompt-assisted and geometry-guided learning)
backbone_modifications: Connector (two-layer MLPs) between visual encoder and LLM

pretraining_phases.dataset: refGeo (integrates RSVG, DIOR-RSVG, GeoChat, VRSBench, and new aerial vehicle dataset)

short_description: GeoLangBind is an agglomerative vision?language foundation model for Earth Observation (EO) that unifies heterogeneous EO data modalities (RGB, SAR, multispectral, hyperspectral, infrared, elevation, aerial) into a shared language embedding space. It leverages a large-scale multimodal image?text dataset (GeoLangBind-2M, 2M pairs, 6 modalities), a wavelength-aware dynamic encoder, a modality-aware knowledge agglomeration (MaKA) module, and progressive multimodal weight merging. GeoLangBind achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot and fine-tuned performance on classification, segmentation, and cross-modal retrieval across 23 EO datasets.
backbone: ViT-Base (patch size 16) for base, SoViT-400m (patch size 14) for large
pretext_training_type: Contrastive vision?language pretraining with multi-teacher knowledge distillation
masking_strategy: Progressive multimodal weight merging; dynamic patch embedding with wavelength-aware masking
pretraining: Initialized with SigLIP weights, trained on GeoLangBind-2M using pairwise sigmoid loss and multi-teacher feature-matching distillation (SigLIP, DINOv2, ViT)

regions_coverage: Global (datasets cover diverse geographies, e.g., Sentinel-2, Sentinel-1, EnMAP, aerial, elevation, infrared)
pretraining_phases.sampling: Dataset split into RGB and non-RGB modalities for progressive weight merging

short_description: SkyCLIP is a remote-sensing-specialized CLIP model developed via continual pre-training on the large-scale, semantically diverse SkyScript vision-language dataset for remote sensing. It outperforms baseline models in zero-shot scene classification, fine-grained attribute classification, and cross-modal retrieval.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive image-text learning
pretraining: Continual pre-training of CLIP initialized from OpenAI weights on the SkyScript dataset using image-text contrastive learning.

regions_coverage: Global (except Antarctica), with high-resolution concentration in US and Europe
pretraining_phases.image_resolution: Variable, 0.1m to 30m GSD, images resized to 224x224 or variable
pretraining_phases.sampling: Top 50% of image-text pairs by CLIP similarity

short_description: SARATR-X is the first foundation model for SAR automatic target recognition (ATR), trained via self-supervised learning on the largest publicly available SAR dataset (SARDet-180K) with a HiViT backbone and a two-step masked image modeling strategy, achieving state-of-the-art performance on classification and detection tasks.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning (Masked Image Modeling, two-step: ImageNet then SAR)
masking_strategy: Masked image modeling (MIM) with patch dropping and multi-scale gradient features as guide signals
pretraining: Two-step pre-training: first on ImageNet with MIM for initialization, then on SARDet-180K SAR dataset with MIM using multi-scale gradient features to suppress speckle noise
domain_knowledge: Multi-scale gradient features for speckle noise suppression
backbone_modifications: Hierarchical structure for high spatial resolution
Small (4x4) input patches for small target recognition

processing: cropping large images to extract small targets
pretraining_phases.sampling: Resampling and hard-sample mining to alleviate class imbalance

benchmarks.application: SAR vehicle, ship, and aircraft recognition (few-shot and robust settings)
SAR vehicle recognition (standard and extended operating conditions)

short_description: IaI-SimCLR is a multi-modal, multi-objective contrastive learning framework for Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 imagery, enforcing both intra- and inter-modality similarity and introducing a local batch sampling strategy for harder negatives.
pretext_training_type: Contrastive self-supervised learning (SimCLR-based) with multi-objective (intra- and inter-modality) losses
pretraining: Self-supervised contrastive learning with intra- and inter-modality objectives, using Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 data, with both random and local batch sampling strategies.
domain_knowledge: Augmentation strategies tailored for remote sensing
backbone_modifications: Separate projection heads for intra- and inter-modality losses

pretraining_phases.sampling: Random Batch Sampling (RBS) and Local Batch Sampling (LBS)

short_description: MTP (Multi-Task Pretraining) is a remote sensing foundation model paradigm that uses a shared encoder and task-specific decoders for multi-task supervised pretraining on the SAMRS dataset, covering semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and rotated object detection. It supports both CNN and vision transformer backbones with over 300M parameters and demonstrates state-of-the-art performance across 14 remote sensing benchmarks.
pretext_training_type: Multi-task supervised pretraining
pretraining: Stage-wise multi-task supervised pretraining on SAMRS dataset with semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and rotated object detection tasks using a shared encoder and task-specific decoders.
backbone_modifications: RVSA: Rotated Varied-Size window Attention for ViT

pretraining_phases.dataset: SAMRS (Segment Anything Model annotated Remote Sensing Segmentation dataset)

benchmarks.application: Land use and land cover classification

short_description: FedSense is a privacy-preserved federated self-supervised learning framework for collaborative pre-training of remote sensing foundation models (RSFMs) across multiple institutions without sharing private data. It introduces Federated Mutual-guidance Learning, including Server-to-Clients Guidance (SCG) and Clients-to-Server Guidance (CSG), to address model drift from data heterogeneity and high communication overhead.
pretext_training_type: Self-supervised learning (contrastive learning and masked image modeling) in federated setting
masking_strategy: Masked image modeling (SimMIM), contrastive learning (DINO)
pretraining: Federated self-supervised pre-training with mutual-guidance learning (SCG and CSG) across 10 clients with heterogeneous remote sensing data and a public dataset on the server

pretraining_phases.dataset: Multi-source federated dataset (see Table 7): 10 clients with private remote sensing data (satellite and aerial), plus public WorldView-3/4 data on server

short_description: Masked Angle-Aware Autoencoder (MA3E) is a self-supervised representation learning model for remote sensing images that introduces explicit angle variation via a scaling center crop operation and reconstructs both original pixels and angle information using an optimal transport loss.
pretext_training_type: Masked image modeling with angle restoration
masking_strategy: Random masking of rotated crop and background at 75% ratio each
pretraining: Self-supervised masked image modeling with explicit angle variation and optimal transport loss for rotated crop reconstruction
supported_sensors: Various (MillionAID is collected from Google Earth, multiple sensors)

short_description: Galileo is a highly multimodal transformer model for remote sensing, capable of learning global and local features from diverse modalities (optical, SAR, elevation, weather, pseudo-labels, etc.) across space and time. It uses a dual self-supervised learning algorithm with global and local contrastive losses and is pretrained on a globally sampled, highly multimodal dataset. Galileo outperforms state-of-the-art specialist models on a wide range of remote sensing benchmarks.
pretext_training_type: Dual local-global self-supervised masked modeling with contrastive losses
masking_strategy: Structured space-time masking for global features; unstructured random masking for local features
pretraining: Masked modeling with dual objectives: global (deep targets, structured masking) and local (shallow targets, unstructured masking) using PatchDisc contrastive loss. Pretrained on a globally sampled, highly multimodal dataset covering 9 remote sensing modalities.
backbone_modifications: Custom tokenization for spatial, temporal, and channel group dimensions
Sinusoidal spatial/temporal, learnable channel, and month embeddings
bands: Sentinel-2: all except B1, B9, B10

pretraining_phases.dataset: Galileo pretraining dataset (globally sampled, 127155 instances, 9 RS modalities)
pretraining_phases.sampling: k-means clustering on WorldCover classes and lat/lon for semantic and geographic diversity
